      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "1421: - Migrated `pod-security-admission` to use [contextual logging](https://k8s.io/docs/concepts/cluster-administration/system-logs/#contextual-logging). ([#114471](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/114471), [@Namanl2001](https://github.com/Namanl2 ... [line truncated]\n1422: - Migrated controller functions to use [contextual logging](https://k8s.io/docs/concepts/cluster-administration/system-logs/#contextual-logging). ([#116930](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/116930), [@fatsheep9146](https://github.com/fatsheep9 ... [line truncated]\n1423: - Migrated the Job controller (within `kube-controller-manager`) to use [contextual logging](https://k8s.io/docs/concepts/cluster-administration/system-logs/#contextual-logging). ([#116910](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/116910), [@fatsheep9 ... [line truncated]\n1424: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 1427,
            "startLine": 1421
          },
          {
            "content": "2264: - Kubernetes is now built with Go 1.20.6 ([#119324](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/119324), [@xmudrii](https://github.com/xmudrii)) [SIG API Machinery, Auth, Cloud Provider, Release and Testing]\n2265: - Migrate `pkg/controller/endpoint` to contextual logging ([#116755](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/116755), [@my-git9](https://github.com/my-git9)) [SIG Apps, Instrumentation and Network]\n2266: - Migrated the `EndpointSlice` and `EndpointSliceMirroring` controllers (within `kube-controller-manager`) to use [contextual logging](https://k8s.io/docs/concepts/cluster-administration/system-logs/#contextual-logging). ([#115295](https://github.com/kuber ... [line truncated]\n2267: - Move non-graceful node shutdown to GA. ([#118228](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/118228), [@carlory](https://github.com/carlory)) [SIG Apps, Storage and Testing]\n2268: ",
            "endLine": 2268,
            "startLine": 2264
          },
          {
            "content": "1682: - `kube-apiserver`: Improved memory use when performing `GetList` on the cache. ([#116327](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/116327), [@sxllwx](https://github.com/sxllwx))\n1683: - `kube-controller-manager` and `cloud-controller-manager` have changed the\n1684:   name of controllers that can be turned on/off that are passed to the `--controllers`\n1685:   flag (e.g., `pod-garbage-collector-controller`). The old names (eg `podgc`) are\n1686:   also accepted and aliased to the new names. ([#115813](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/115813), [@atiratree](https://github.com/atiratree))\n1687: ",
            "endLine": 1687,
            "startLine": 1682
          }

      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "2785: - E2e tests wait for `kube-root-ca.crt` to be populated in namespaces for use with projected service account tokens, reducing delays starting those test pods and errors in the logs. ([#107763](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/107763), [@smarte ... [line truncated]\n2786: - Endpoints and EndpointSlice controllers no longer populate [resourceVersion of targetRef](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/kubernetes-api/common-definitions/object-reference/#ObjectReference) in Endpoints and EndpointSlices ([#108450](https://github. ... [line truncated]\n2787: - Fixed default config flags for `NewDefaultKubectlCommand`. ([#107131](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/107131), [@jonnylangefeld](https://github.com/jonnylangefeld))\n2788: ",
            "endLine": 2788,
            "startLine": 2785
          },
          {
            "content": "3536: - API server's deprecated `--target-ram-mb` flag is now removed. ([#108457](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/108457), [@ialidzhikov](https://github.com/ialidzhikov)) [SIG API Machinery, Cloud Provider, Scalability and Testing]\n3537: - Endpoints and EndpointSlice controllers no longer populate [resourceVersion of targetRef](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/kubernetes-api/common-definitions/object-reference/#ObjectReference) in Endpoints and EndpointSlices ([#108450](https://github. ... [line truncated]\n3538: - Improve error message when applying CRDs before the CRD exists in a cluster ([#107363](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/107363), [@eddiezane](https://github.com/eddiezane)) [SIG CLI]\n3539: ",
            "endLine": 3539,
            "startLine": 3536
          },
          {
            "content": "2551: - The `ServiceLBNodePortControl` feature has graduated to GA. The feature gate will be removed in 1.26. ([#107027](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/107027), [@uablrek](https://github.com/uablrek))\n2552: - The deprecated kube-controller-manager flag '--deployment-controller-sync-period' has been removed, it is not used by the deployment controller. ([#107178](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/107178), [@SataQiu](https://github.com/SataQiu))\n2553: - The feature `DynamicKubeletConfig` has been removed from the kubelet. ([#106932](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/106932), [@SergeyKanzhelev](https://github.com/SergeyKanzhelev))\n2554: ",
            "endLine": 2554,
            "startLine": 2551
          }
        ],

\"isDirectory\":true,\"path\":\"pkg/controller/ttlafterfinished/\"},{\"isDirectory\":true,\"path\":\"pkg/controller/util/\"},{\"isDirectory\":true,\"path\":\"pkg/controller/validatingadmissionpolicystatus/\"},{\"isDirectory\":true,\"path\":\"pkg/controller/volume/\"},{\"isDirectory\":false,\"path\":\"pkg/controller/OWNERS\"},{\"isDirectory\":false,\"path\":\"pkg/controller/controller_ref_manager.go\"},{\"isDirectory\":false,\"path\":\"pkg/controller/controller_ref_manager_test.go\"},{\"isDirectory\":false,\"path\":\"pkg/controller/controller_utils.go\"},{\"isDirectory\":false,\"path\":\"pkg/controller/controller_utils_test.go\"},{\"isDirectory\":false,\"path\":\"pkg/controller/doc.go\"}]}",

\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"time\\\"\\n23: \\n24: \\t\\\"golang.org/x/time/rate\\\"\\n25: \\n26: \\tv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n27: \\tdiscovery \\\"k8s.io/api/discovery/v1\\\"\\n28: \\tapierrors \\tdiscoveryinformers \\\"k8s.io/client-go/informers/discovery/v1\\\"\\n35: \\tclientset \\\"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes\\\"\\n36: \\t\\\"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/scheme\\\"\\n37: \\tv1core \\\"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/typed/core/v1\\\"\\n38: \\tcorelisters \\\"k8s.io/client-go/listers/core/v1\\\"\\n39: \\tdiscoverylisters \\\"k8s.io/client-go/listers/discovery/v1\\\"\\n40: \\t\\\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache\\\"\\n41: \\t\\\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record\\\"\\n42: \\t\\\"k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue\\\"\\n43: \\tendpointslicerec \\\"k8s.io/endpointslice\\\"\\n44: \\tendpointslicemetrics \\\"k8s.io/endpointslice/metrics\\\"\\n45: \\t\\\"k8s.io/endpointslice/topologycache\\\"\\n46: \\tendpointsliceutil \\\"k8s.io/endpointslice/util\\\"\\n47: \\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n48: \\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller\\\"\\n49: \\tendpointslicepkg \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller/util/endpointslice\\\"\\n50: \\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/features\\\"\\n51: )\\n52: \\n53: const (\\n54: \\t// maxRetries is the number of times a service will be retried before it is\\n55: \\t// dropped out of the queue. Any sync error, such as a failure to create or\\n56: \\t// update an EndpointSlice could trigger a retry. With the current\\n57: \\t// rate-limiter in use (1s*2^(numRetries-1)) the following numbers represent\\n58: \\t// the sequence of delays between successive queuings of a service.\\n59: \\t//\\n60: \\t// 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 32s, 64s, 128s, 256s, 512s, 1000s (max)\\n61: \\tmaxRetries = 15\\n62: \\n63: \\t// endpointSliceChangeMinSyncDelay indicates the minimum delay before\\n64: \\t// queuing a syncService call after an EndpointSlice changes. If\\n65: \\t// endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod is greater than this value, it will be used\\n66: \\t// instead. This helps batch processing of changes to multiple\\n67: \\t// EndpointSlices.\\n68: \\tendpointSliceChangeMinSyncDelay = 1 * time.Second\\n69: \\n70: \\t// defaultSyncBackOff is the default backoff period for syncService calls.\\n71: \\tdefaultSyncBackOff = 1 * time.Second\\n72: \\t// maxSyncBackOff is the max backoff period for syncService calls.\\n73: \\tmaxSyncBackOff = 1000 * time.Second\\n74: \\n75: \\t// controllerName is a unique value used with LabelManagedBy to indicated\\n76: \\t// the component managing an EndpointSlice.\\n77: \\tcontrollerName = \\\"endpointslice-controller.k8s.io\\\"\\n78: )\\n79: \\n80: // NewController creates and initializes a new Controller\\n81: func NewController(ctx context.Context, podInformer coreinformers.PodInformer,\\n82: \\tserviceInformer coreinformers.ServiceInformer,\\n83: \\tnodeInformer coreinformers.NodeInformer,\\n84: \\tendpointSliceInformer discoveryinformers.EndpointSliceInformer,\\n85: \\tmaxEndpointsPerSlice int32,\\n86: \\tclient clientset.Interface,\\n87: \\tendpointUpdatesBatchPeriod time.Duration,\\n88: ) *Controller {\\n89: \\tbroadcaster := record.NewBroadcaster(record.WithContext(ctx))\\n90: \\trecorder := broadcaster.NewRecorder(scheme.Scheme, v1.EventSource{Component: \\\"endpoint-slice-controller\\\"})\\n91: \\n92: \\tendpointslicemetrics.RegisterMetrics()\\n93: \\n94: \\tc := &Controller{\\n95: \\t\\tclient: client,\\n96: \\t\\t// This is similar to the DefaultControllerRateLimiter, just with a\\n97: \\t\\t// significantly higher default backoff (1s vs 5ms). This controller\\n98: \\t\\t// processes events that can require significant EndpointSlice changes,\\n99: \\t\\t// such as an update to a Service or Deployment. A more significant\\n100: \\t\\t// rate limit back off here helps ensure that the Controller does not\\n101: \\t\\t// overwhelm the API Server.\\n102: \\t\\tqueue: workqueue.NewNamedRateLimitingQueue(workqueue.NewMaxOfRateLimiter(\\n103: \\t\\t\\tworkqueue.NewItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter(defaultSyncBackOff, maxSyncBackOff),\\n104: \\t\\t\\t// 10 qps, 100 bucket size. This is only for retry speed and its\\n105: \\t\\t\\t// only the overall factor (not per item).\\n106: \\t\\t\\t&workqueue.BucketRateLimiter{Limiter: rate.NewLimiter(rate.Limit(10), 100)},\\n107: \\t\\t), \\\"endpoint_slice\\\"),\\n108: \\t\\tworkerLoopPeriod: time.Second,\\n109: \\t}\\n110: \\n111: \\tserviceInformer.Informer().AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{\\n112: \\t\\tAddFunc: c.onServiceUpdate,\\n113: \\t\\tUpdateFunc: func(old, cur interface{}) {\\n114: \\t\\t\\tc.onServiceUpdate(cur)\\n115: \\t\\t},\\n116: \\t\\tDeleteFunc: c.onServiceDelete,\\n117: \\t})\\n118: \\tc.serviceLister = serviceInformer.Lister()\\n119: \\tc.servicesSynced = serviceInformer.Informer().HasSynced\\n120: \\n121: \\tpodInformer.Informer().AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{\\n122: \\t\\tAddFunc:    c.addPod,\\n123: \\t\\tUpdateFunc: c.updatePod,\\n124: \\t\\tDeleteFunc: c.deletePod,\\n125: \\t})\\n126: \\tc.podLister = podInformer.Lister()\\n127: \\tc.podsSynced = podInformer.Informer().HasSynced\\n128: \\n129: \\tc.nodeLister = nodeInformer.Lister()\\n130: \\tc.nodesSynced = nodeInformer.Informer().HasSynced\\n131: \\n132: \\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\\n133: \\tendpointSliceInformer.Informer().AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{\\n134: \\t\\tAddFunc: c.onEndpointSliceAdd,\\n135: \\t\\tUpdateFunc: func(oldObj, newObj interface{}) {\\n136: \\t\\t\\tc.onEndpointSliceUpdate(logger, oldObj, newObj)\\n137: \\t\\t},\\n138: \\t\\tDeleteFunc: c.onEndpointSliceDelete,\\n139: \\t})\\n140: \\n141: \\tc.endpointSliceLister = endpointSliceInformer.Lister()\\n142: \\tc.endpointSlicesSynced = endpointSliceInformer.Informer().HasSynced\\n143: \\tc.endpointSliceTracker = endpointsliceutil.NewEndpointSliceTracker()\\n144: \\n145: \\tc.maxEndpointsPerSlice = maxEndpointsPerSlice\\n146: \\n147: \\tc.triggerTimeTracker = endpointsliceutil.NewTriggerTimeTracker()\\n148: \\n149: \\tc.eventBroadcaster = broadcaster\\n150: \\tc.eventRecorder = recorder\\n151: \\n152: \\tc.endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod = endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod\\n153: \\n154: \\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.TopologyAwareHints) {\\n155: \\t\\tnodeInformer.Informer().AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{\\n156: \\t\\t\\tAddFunc: func(obj interface{}) {\\n157: \\t\\t\\t\\tc.addNode(logger, obj)\\n158: \\t\\t\\t},\\n159: \\t\\t\\tUpdateFunc: func(oldObj, newObj interface{}) {\\n160: \\t\\t\\t\\tc.updateNode(logger, oldObj, newObj)\\n161: \\t\\t\\t},\\n162: \\t\\t\\tDeleteFunc: func(obj interface{}) {\\n163: \\t\\t\\t\\tc.deleteNode(logger, obj)\\n164: \\t\\t\\t},\\n165: \\t\\t})\\n166: \\n167: \\t\\tc.topologyCache = topologycache.NewTopologyCache()\\n168: \\t}\\n169: \\n170: \\tc.reconciler = endpointslicerec.NewReconciler(\\n171: \\t\\tc.client,\\n172: \\t\\tc.nodeLister,\\n173: \\t\\tc.maxEndpointsPerSlice,\\n174: \\t\\tc.endpointSliceTracker,\\n175: \\t\\tc.topologyCache,\\n176: \\t\\tc.eventRecorder,\\n177: \\t\\tcontrollerName,\\n178: \\t\\tendpointslicerec.WithTrafficDistributionEnabled(utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.ServiceTrafficDistribution)),\\n179: \\t)\\n180: \\n181: \\treturn c\\n182: }\\n183: \\n184: // Controller manages selector-based service endpoint slices\\n185: type Controller struct {\\n186: \\tclient           clientset.Interface\\n187: \\teventBroadcaster record.EventBroadcaster\\n188: \\teventRecorder    record.EventRecorder\\n189: \\n190: \\t// serviceLister is able to list/get services and is populated by the\\n191: \\t// shared informer passed to NewController\\n192: \\tserviceLister corelisters.ServiceLister\\n193: \\t// servicesSynced returns true if the service shared informer has been synced at least once.\\n194: \\t// Added as a member to the struct to allow injection for testing.\\n195: \\tservicesSynced cache.InformerSynced\\n196: \\n197: \\t// podLister is able to list/get pods and is populated by the\\n198: \\t// shared informer passed to NewController\\n199: \\tpodLister corelisters.PodLister\\n200: \\t// podsSynced returns true if the pod shared informer has been synced at least once.\\n201: \\t// Added as a member to the struct to allow injection for testing.\\n202: \\tpodsSynced cache.InformerSynced\\n203: \\n204: \\t// endpointSliceLister is able to list/get endpoint slices and is populated by the\\n205: \\t// shared informer passed to NewController\\n206: \\tendpointSliceLister discoverylisters.EndpointSliceLister\\n207: \\t// endpointSlicesSynced returns true if the endpoint slice shared informer has been synced at least once.\\n208: \\t// Added as a member to the struct to allow injection for testing.\\n209: \\tendpointSlicesSynced cache.InformerSynced\\n210: \\t// endpointSliceTracker tracks the list of EndpointSlices and associated\\n211: \\t// resource versions expected for each Service. It can help determine if a\\n212: \\t// cached EndpointSlice is out of date.\\n213: \\tendpointSliceTracker *endpointsliceutil.EndpointSliceTracker\\n214: \\n215: \\t// nodeLister is able to list/get nodes and is populated by the\\n216: \\t// shared informer passed to NewController\\n217: \\tnodeLister corelisters.NodeLister\\n218: \\t// nodesSynced returns true if the node shared informer has been synced at least once.\\n219: \\t// Added as a member to the struct to allow injection for testing.\\n220: \\tnodesSynced cache.InformerSynced\\n221: \\n222: \\t// reconciler is an util used to reconcile EndpointSlice changes.\\n223: \\treconciler *endpointslicerec.Reconciler\\n224: \\n225: \\t// triggerTimeTracker is an util used to compute and export the\\n226: \\t// EndpointsLastChangeTriggerTime annotation.\\n227: \\ttriggerTimeTracker *endpointsliceutil.TriggerTimeTracker\\n228: \\n229: \\t// Services that need to be updated. A channel is inappropriate here,\\n230: \\t// because it allows services with lots of pods to be serviced much\\n231: \\t// more often than services with few pods; it also would cause a\\n232: \\t// service that's inserted multiple times to be processed more than\\n233: \\t// necessary.\\n234: \\tqueue workqueue.RateLimitingInterface\\n235: \\n236: \\t// maxEndpointsPerSlice references the maximum number of endpoints that\\n237: \\t// should be added to an EndpointSlice\\n238: \\tmaxEndpointsPerSlice int32\\n239: \\n240: \\t// workerLoopPeriod is the time between worker runs. The workers\\n241: \\t// process the queue of service and pod changes\\n242: \\tworkerLoopPeriod time.Duration\\n243: \\n244: \\t// endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod is an artificial delay added to all service syncs triggered by pod changes.\\n245: \\t// This can be used to reduce overall number of all endpoint slice updates.\\n246: \\tendpointUpdatesBatchPeriod time.Duration\\n247: \\n248: \\t// topologyCache tracks the distribution of Nodes and endpoints across zones\\n249: \\t// to enable TopologyAwareHints.\\n250: \\ttopologyCache *topologycache.TopologyCache\\n251: }\\n252: \\n253: // Run will not return until stopCh is closed.\\n254: func (c *Controller) Run(ctx context.Context, workers int) {\\n255: \\tdefer utilruntime.HandleCrash()\\n256: \\n257: \\t// Start events processing pipeline.\\n258: \\tc.eventBroadcaster.StartLogging(klog.Infof)\\n259: \\tc.eventBroadcaster.StartRecordingToSink(&v1core.EventSinkImpl{Interface: c.client.CoreV1().Events(\\\"\\\")})\\n260: \\tdefer c.eventBroadcaster.Shutdown()\\n261: \\n262: \\tdefer c.queue.ShutDown()\\n263: \\n264: \\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\\n265: \\tlogger.Info(\\\"Starting endpoint slice controller\\\")\\n266: \\tdefer logger.Info(\\\"Shutting down endpoint slice controller\\\")\\n267: \\n268: \\tif !cache.WaitForNamedCacheSync(\\\"endpoint_slice\\\", ctx.Done(), c.podsSynced, c.servicesSynced, c.endpointSlicesSynced, c.nodesSynced) {\\n269: \\t\\treturn\\n270: \\t}\\n271: \\n272: \\tlogger.V(2).Info(\\\"Starting worker threads\\\", \\\"total\\\", workers)\\n273: \\tfor i := 0; i < workers; i++ {\\n274: \\t\\tgo wait.Until(func() { c.worker(logger) }, c.workerLoopPeriod, ctx.Done())\\n275: \\t}\\n276: \\n277: \\t<-ctx.Done()\\n278: }\\n279: \\n280: // worker runs a worker thread that just dequeues items, processes them, and\\n281: // marks them done. You may run as many of these in parallel as you wish; the\\n282: // workqueue guarantees that they will not end up processing the same service\\n283: // at the same time\\n284: func (c *Controller) worker(logger klog.Logger) {\\n285: \\tfor c.processNextWorkItem(logger) {\\n286: \\t}\\n287: }\\n288: \\n289: func (c *Controller) processNextWorkItem(logger klog.Logger) bool {\\n290: \\tcKey, quit := c.queue.Get()\\n291: \\tif quit {\\n292: \\t\\treturn false\\n293: \\t}\\n294: \\tdefer c.queue.Done(cKey)\\n295: \\n296: \\terr := c.syncService(logger, cKey.(string))\\n297: \\tc.handleErr(logger, err, cKey)\\n298: \\n299: \\treturn true\\n300: }\\n301: \\n302: func (c *Controller) handleErr(logger klog.Logger, err error, key interface{}) {\\n303: \\ttrackSync(err)\\n304: \\n305: \\tif err == nil {\\n306: \\t\\tc.queue.Forget(key)\\n307: \\t\\treturn\\n308: \\t}\\n309: \\n310: \\tif c.queue.NumRequeues(key) < maxRetries {\\n311: \\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Error syncing endpoint slices for service, retrying\\\", \\\"key\\\", key, \\\"err\\\", err)\\n312: \\t\\tc.queue.AddRateLimited(key)\\n313: \\t\\treturn\\n314: \\t}\\n315: \\n316: \\tlogger.Info(\\\"Retry budget exceeded, dropping service out of the queue\\\", \\\"key\\\", key, \\\"err\\\", err)\\n317: \\tc.queue.Forget(key)\\n318: \\tutilruntime.HandleError(err)\\n319: }\\n320: \\n321: func (c *Controller) syncService(logger klog.Logger, key string) error {\\n322: \\tstartTime := time.Now()\\n323: \\tdefer func() {\\n324: \\t\\tlogger.V(4).Info(\\\"Finished syncing service endpoint slices\\\", \\\"key\\\", key, \\\"elapsedTime\\\", time.Since(startTime))\\n325: \\t}()\\n326: \\n327: \\tnamespace, name, err := cache.SplitMetaNamespaceKey(key)\\n328: \\tif err != nil {\\n329: \\t\\treturn err\\n330: \\t}\\n331: \\n332: \\tservice, err := c.serviceLister.Services(namespace).Get(name)\\n333: \\tif err != nil {\\n334: \\t\\tif !apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {\\n335: \\t\\t\\treturn err\\n336: \\t\\t}\\n337: \\n338: \\t\\tc.triggerTimeTracker.DeleteService(namespace, name)\\n339: \\t\\tc.reconciler.DeleteService(namespace, name)\\n340: \\t\\tc.endpointSliceTracker.DeleteService(namespace, name)\\n341: \\t\\t// The service has been deleted, return nil so that it won't be retried.\\n342: \\t\\treturn nil\\n343: \\t}\\n344: \\n345: \\tif service.Spec.Type == v1.ServiceTypeExternalName {\\n346: \\t\\t// services with Type ExternalName receive no endpoints from this controller;\\n347: \\t\\t// Ref: https://issues.k8s.io/105986\\n348: \\t\\treturn nil\\n349: \\t}\\n350: \\n351: \\tif service.Spec.Selector == nil {\\n352: \\t\\t// services without a selector receive no endpoint slices from this controller;\\n353: \\t\\t// these services will receive endpoint slices that are created out-of-band via the REST API.\\n354: \\t\\treturn nil\\n355: \\t}\\n356: \\n357: \\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"About to update endpoint slices for service\\\", \\\"key\\\", key)\\n358: \\n359: \\tpodLabelSelector := labels.Set(service.Spec.Selector).AsSelectorPreValidated()\\n360: \\tpods, err := c.podLister.Pods(service.Namespace).List(podLabelSelector)\\n361: \\tif err != nil {\\n362: \\t\\t// Since we're getting stuff from a local cache, it is basically\\n363: \\t\\t// impossible to get this error.\\n364: \\t\\tc.eventRecorder.Eventf(service, v1.EventTypeWarning, \\\"FailedToListPods\\\",\\n365: \\t\\t\\t\\\"Error listing Pods for Service %s/%s: %v\\\", service.Namespace, service.Name, err)\\n366: \\t\\treturn err\\n367: \\t}\\n368: \\n369: \\tesLabelSelector := labels.Set(map[string]string{\\n370: \\t\\tdiscovery.LabelServiceName: service.Name,\\n371: \\t\\tdiscovery.LabelManagedBy:   c.reconciler.GetControllerName(),\\n372: \\t}).AsSelectorPreValidated()\\n373: \\tendpointSlices, err := c.endpointSliceLister.EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).List(esLabelSelector)\\n374: \\n375: \\tif err != nil {\\n376: \\t\\t// Since we're getting stuff from a local cache, it is basically\\n377: \\t\\t// impossible to get this error.\\n378: \\t\\tc.eventRecorder.Eventf(service, v1.EventTypeWarning, \\\"FailedToListEndpointSlices\\\",\\n379: \\t\\t\\t\\\"Error listing Endpoint Slices for Service %s/%s: %v\\\", service.Namespace, service.Name, err)\\n380: \\t\\treturn err\\n381: \\t}\\n382: \\n383: \\t// Drop EndpointSlices that have been marked for deletion to prevent the controller from getting stuck.\\n384: \\tendpointSlices = dropEndpointSlicesPendingDeletion(endpointSlices)\\n385: \\n386: \\tif c.endpointSliceTracker.StaleSlices(service, endpointSlices) {\\n387: \\t\\treturn endpointslicepkg.NewStaleInformerCache(\\\"EndpointSlice informer cache is out of date\\\")\\n388: \\t}\\n389: \\n390: \\t// We call ComputeEndpointLastChangeTriggerTime here to make sure that the\\n391: \\t// state of the trigger time tracker gets updated even if the sync turns out\\n392: \\t// to be no-op and we don't update the EndpointSlice objects.\\n393: \\tlastChangeTriggerTime := c.triggerTimeTracker.\\n394: \\t\\tComputeEndpointLastChangeTriggerTime(namespace, service, pods)\\n395: \\n396: \\terr = c.reconciler.Reconcile(logger, service, pods, endpointSlices, lastChangeTriggerTime)\\n397: \\tif err != nil {\\n398: \\t\\tc.eventRecorder.Eventf(service, v1.EventTypeWarning, \\\"FailedToUpdateEndpointSlices\\\",\\n399: \\t\\t\\t\\\"Error updating Endpoint Slices for Service %s/%s: %v\\\", service.Namespace, service.Name, err)\\n400: \\t\\treturn err\\n401: \\t}\\n402: \\n403: \\treturn nil\\n404: }\\n405: \\n406: // onServiceUpdate updates the Service Selector in the cache and queues the Service for processing.\\n407: func (c *Controller) onServiceUpdate(obj interface{}) {\\n408: \\tkey, err := controller.KeyFunc(obj)\\n409: \\tif err != nil {\\n410: \\t\\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"Couldn't get key for object %+v: %v\\\", obj, err))\\n411: \\t\\treturn\\n412: \\t}\\n413: \\n414: \\tc.queue.Add(key)\\n415: }\\n416: \\n417: // onServiceDelete removes the Service Selector from the cache and queues the Service for processing.\\n418: func (c *Controller) onServiceDelete(obj interface{}) {\\n419: \\tkey, err := controller.KeyFunc(obj)\\n420: \\tif err != nil {\\n421: \\t\\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"Couldn't get key for object %+v: %v\\\", obj, err))\\n422: \\t\\treturn\\n423: \\t}\\n424: \\n425: \\tc.queue.Add(key)\\n426: }\\n427: \\n428: // onEndpointSliceAdd queues a sync for the relevant Service for a sync if the\\n429: // EndpointSlice resource version does not match the expected version in the\\n430: // endpointSliceTracker.\\n431: func (c *Controller) onEndpointSliceAdd(obj interface{}) {\\n432: \\tendpointSlice := obj.(*discovery.EndpointSlice)\\n433: \\tif endpointSlice == nil {\\n434: \\t\\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"Invalid EndpointSlice provided to onEndpointSliceAdd()\\\"))\\n435: \\t\\treturn\\n436: \\t}\\n437: \\tif c.reconciler.ManagedByController(endpointSlice) && c.endpointSliceTracker.ShouldSync(endpointSlice) {\\n438: \\t\\tc.queueServiceForEndpointSlice(endpointSlice)\\n439: \\t}\\n440: }\\n441: \\n442: // onEndpointSliceUpdate queues a sync for the relevant Service for a sync if\\n443: // the EndpointSlice resource version does not match the expected version in the\\n444: // endpointSliceTracker or the managed-by value of the EndpointSlice has changed\\n445: // from or to this controller.\\n446: func (c *Controller) onEndpointSliceUpdate(logger klog.Logger, prevObj, obj interface{}) {\\n447: \\tprevEndpointSlice := prevObj.(*discovery.EndpointSlice)\\n448: \\tendpointSlice := obj.(*discovery.EndpointSlice)\\n449: \\tif endpointSlice == nil || prevEndpointSlice == nil {\\n450: \\t\\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"Invalid EndpointSlice provided to onEndpointSliceUpdate()\\\"))\\n451: \\t\\treturn\\n452: \\t}\\n453: \\t// EndpointSlice generation does not change when labels change. Although the\\n454: \\t// controller will never change LabelServiceName, users might. This check\\n455: \\t// ensures that we handle changes to this label.\\n456: \\tsvcName := endpointSlice.Labels[discovery.LabelServiceName]\\n457: \\tprevSvcName := prevEndpointSlice.Labels[discovery.LabelServiceName]\\n458: \\tif svcName != prevSvcName {\\n459: \\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"label changed\\\", \\\"label\\\", discovery.LabelServiceName, \\\"oldService\\\", prevSvcName, \\\"newService\\\", svcName, \\\"endpointslice\\\", klog.KObj(endpointSlice))\\n460: \\t\\tc.queueServiceForEndpointSlice(endpointSlice)\\n461: \\t\\tc.queueServiceForEndpointSlice(prevEndpointSlice)\\n462: \\t\\treturn\\n463: \\t}\\n464: \\tif c.reconciler.ManagedByChanged(prevEndpointSlice, endpointSlice) || (c.reconciler.ManagedByController(endpointSlice) && c.endpointSliceTracker.ShouldSync(endpointSlice)) {\\n465: \\t\\tc.queueServiceForEndpointSlice(endpointSlice)\\n466: \\t}\\n467: }\\n468: \\n469: // onEndpointSliceDelete queues a sync for the relevant Service for a sync if the\\n470: // EndpointSlice resource version does not match the expected version in the\\n471: // endpointSliceTracker.\\n472: func (c *Controller) onEndpointSliceDelete(obj interface{}) {\\n473: \\tendpointSlice := getEndpointSliceFromDeleteAction(obj)\\n474: \\tif endpointSlice != nil && c.reconciler.ManagedByController(endpointSlice) && c.endpointSliceTracker.Has(endpointSlice) {\\n475: \\t\\t// This returns false if we didn't expect the EndpointSlice to be\\n476: \\t\\t// deleted. If that is the case, we queue the Service for another sync.\\n477: \\t\\tif !c.endpointSliceTracker.HandleDeletion(endpointSlice) {\\n478: \\t\\t\\tc.queueServiceForEndpointSlice(endpointSlice)\\n479: \\t\\t}\\n480: \\t}\\n481: }\\n482: \\n483: // queueServiceForEndpointSlice attempts to queue the corresponding Service for\\n484: // the provided EndpointSlice.\\n485: func (c *Controller) queueServiceForEndpointSlice(endpointSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice) {\\n486: \\tkey, err := endpointslicerec.ServiceControllerKey(endpointSlice)\\n487: \\tif err != nil {\\n488: \\t\\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"Couldn't get key for EndpointSlice %+v: %v\\\", endpointSlice, err))\\n489: \\t\\treturn\\n490: \\t}\\n491: \\n492: \\t// queue after the max of endpointSliceChangeMinSyncDelay and\\n493: \\t// endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod.\\n494: \\tdelay := endpointSliceChangeMinSyncDelay\\n495: \\tif c.endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod > delay {\\n496: \\t\\tdelay = c.endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod\\n497: \\t}\\n498: \\tc.queue.AddAfter(key, delay)\\n499: }\\n500: \\n501: func (c *Controller) addPod(obj interface{}) {\\n502: \\tpod := obj.(*v1.Pod)\\n503: \\tservices, err := endpointsliceutil.GetPodServiceMemberships(c.serviceLister, pod)\\n504: \\tif err != nil {\\n505: \\t\\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"Unable to get pod %s/%s's service memberships: %v\\\", pod.Namespace, pod.Name, err))\\n506: \\t\\treturn\\n507: \\t}\\n508: \\tfor key := range services {\\n509: \\t\\tc.queue.AddAfter(key, c.endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod)\\n510: \\t}\\n511: }\\n512: \\n513: func (c *Controller) updatePod(old, cur interface{}) {\\n514: \\tservices := endpointsliceutil.GetServicesToUpdateOnPodChange(c.serviceLister, old, cur)\\n515: \\tfor key := range services {\\n516: \\t\\tc.queue.AddAfter(key, c.endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod)\\n517: \\t}\\n518: }\\n519: \\n520: // When a pod is deleted, enqueue the services the pod used to be a member of\\n521: // obj could be an *v1.Pod, or a DeletionFinalStateUnknown marker item.\\n522: func (c *Controller) deletePod(obj interface{}) {\\n523: \\tpod := endpointsliceutil.GetPodFromDeleteAction(obj)\\n524: \\tif pod != nil {\\n525: \\t\\tc.addPod(pod)\\n526: \\t}\\n527: }\\n528: \\n529: func (c *Controller) addNode(logger klog.Logger, obj interface{}) {\\n530: \\tc.checkNodeTopologyDistribution(logger)\\n531: }\\n532: \\n533: func (c *Controller) updateNode(logger klog.Logger, old, cur interface{}) {\\n534: \\toldNode := old.(*v1.Node)\\n535: \\tcurNode := cur.(*v1.Node)\\n536: \\n537: \\t// LabelTopologyZone may be added by cloud provider asynchronously after the Node is created.\\n538: \\t// The topology cache should be updated in this case.\\n539: \\tif isNodeReady(oldNode) != isNodeReady(curNode) ||\\n540: \\t\\toldNode.Labels[v1.LabelTopologyZone] != curNode.Labels[v1.LabelTopologyZone] {\\n541: \\t\\tc.checkNodeTopologyDistribution(logger)\\n542: \\t}\\n543: }\\n544: \\n545: func (c *Controller) deleteNode(logger klog.Logger, obj interface{}) {\\n546: \\tc.checkNodeTopologyDistribution(logger)\\n547: }\\n548: \\n549: // checkNodeTopologyDistribution updates Nodes in the topology cache and then\\n550: // queues any Services that are past the threshold.\\n551: func (c *Controller) checkNodeTopologyDistribution(logger klog.Logger) {\\n552: \\tif c.topologyCache == nil {\\n553: \\t\\treturn\\n554: \\t}\\n555: \\tnodes, err := c.nodeLister.List(labels.Everything())\\n556: \\tif err != nil {\\n557: \\t\\tlogger.Error(err, \\\"Error listing Nodes\\\")\\n558: \\t\\treturn\\n559: \\t}\\n560: \\tc.topologyCache.SetNodes(logger, nodes)\\n561: \\tserviceKeys := c.topologyCache.GetOverloadedServices()\\n562: \\tfor _, serviceKey := range serviceKeys {\\n563: \\t\\tlogger.V(2).Info(\\\"Queuing Service after Node change due to overloading\\\", \\\"key\\\", serviceKey)\\n564: \\t\\tc.queue.Add(serviceKey)\\n565: \\t}\\n566: }\\n567: \\n568: // trackSync increments the EndpointSliceSyncs metric with the result of a sync.\\n569: func trackSync(err error) {\\n570: \\tmetricLabel := \\\"success\\\"\\n571: \\tif err != nil {\\n572: \\t\\tif endpointslicepkg.IsStaleInformerCacheErr(err) {\\n573: \\t\\t\\tmetricLabel = \\\"stale\\\"\\n574: \\t\\t} else {\\n575: \\t\\t\\tmetricLabel = \\\"error\\\"\\n576: \\t\\t}\\n577: \\t}\\n578: \\tendpointslicemetrics.EndpointSliceSyncs.WithLabelValues(metricLabel).Inc()\\n579: }\\n580: \\n581: func dropEndpointSlicesPendingDeletion(endpointSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice) []*discovery.EndpointSlice {\\n582: \\tn := 0\\n583: \\tfor _, endpointSlice := range endpointSlices {\\n584: \\t\\tif endpointSlice.DeletionTimestamp == nil {\\n585: \\t\\t\\tendpointSlices[n] = endpointSlice\\n586: \\t\\t\\tn++\\n587: \\t\\t}\\n588: \\t}\\n589: \\treturn endpointSlices[:n]\\n590: }\\n591: \\n592: // getEndpointSliceFromDeleteAction parses an EndpointSlice from a delete action.\\n593: func getEndpointSliceFromDeleteAction(obj interface{}) *discovery.EndpointSlice {\\n594: \\tif endpointSlice, ok := obj.(*discovery.EndpointSlice); ok {\\n595: \\t\\t// Enqueue all the services that the pod used to be a member of.\\n596: \\t\\t// This is the same thing we do when we add a pod.\\n597: \\t\\treturn endpointSlice\\n598: \\t}\\n599: \\t// If we reached here it means the pod was deleted but its final state is unrecorded.\\n600: \\ttombstone, ok := obj.(cache.DeletedFinalStateUnknown)\\n601: \\tif !ok {\\n602: \\t\\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"Couldn't get object from tombstone %#v\\\", obj))\\n603: \\t\\treturn nil\\n604: \\t}\\n605: \\tendpointSlice, ok := tombstone.Obj.(*discovery.EndpointSlice)\\n606: \\tif !ok {\\n607: \\t\\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"Tombstone contained object that is not a EndpointSlice: %#v\\\", obj))\\n608: \\t\\treturn nil\\n609: \\t}\\n610: \\treturn endpointSlice\\n611: }\\n612: \\n613: // isNodeReady returns true if a node is ready; false otherwise.\\n614: func isNodeReady(node *v1.Node) bool {\\n615: \\tfor _, c := range node.Status.Conditions {\\n616: \\t\\tif c.Type == v1.NodeReady {\\n617: \\t\\t\\treturn c.Status == v1.ConditionTrue\\n618: \\t\\t}\\n619: \\t}\\n620: \\treturn false\\n621: }\\n622: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/pkg/controller/endpointslice/endpointslice_controller.go\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2019 The Kubernetes Authors.\n3: \n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\n7: \n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9: \n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14: limitations under the License.\n15: */\n16: \n17: package endpointslice\n18: \n19: import (\n20: \t\"context\"\n21: \t\"fmt\"\n22: \t\"time\"\n23: \n24: \t\"golang.org/x/time/rate\"\n25: \n26: \tv1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n27: \tdiscovery \"k8s.io/api/discovery/v1\"\n28: \tapierrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n29: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\"\n30: \tutilruntime \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\"\n31: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait\"\n32: \tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n33: \tcoreinformers \"k8s.io/client-go/informers/core/v1\"\n34: \tdiscoveryinformers \"k8s.io/client-go/informers/discovery/v1\"\n35: \tclientset \"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes\"\n36: \t\"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/scheme\"\n37: \tv1core \"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/typed/core/v1\"\n38: \tcorelisters \"k8s.io/client-go/listers/core/v1\"\n39: \tdiscoverylisters \"k8s.io/client-go/listers/discovery/v1\"\n40: \t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache\"\n41: \t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record\"\n42: \t\"k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue\"\n43: \tendpointslicerec \"k8s.io/endpointslice\"\n44: \tendpointslicemetrics \"k8s.io/endpointslice/metrics\"\n45: \t\"k8s.io/endpointslice/topologycache\"\n46: \tendpointsliceutil \"k8s.io/endpointslice/util\"\n47: \t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n48: \t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller\"\n49: \tendpointslicepkg \"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/controller/util/endpointslice\"\n50: \t\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/features\"\n51: )\n52: \n53: const (\n54: \t// maxRetries is the number of times a service will be retried before it is\n55: \t// dropped out of the queue. Any sync error, such as a failure to create or\n56: \t// update an EndpointSlice could trigger a retry. With the current\n57: \t// rate-limiter in use (1s*2^(numRetries-1)) the following numbers represent\n58: \t// the sequence of delays between successive queuings of a service.\n59: \t//\n60: \t// 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 32s, 64s, 128s, 256s, 512s, 1000s (max)\n61: \tmaxRetries = 15\n62: \n63: \t// endpointSliceChangeMinSyncDelay indicates the minimum delay before\n64: \t// queuing a syncService call after an EndpointSlice changes. If\n65: \t// endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod is greater than this value, it will be used\n66: \t// instead. This helps batch processing of changes to multiple\n67: \t// EndpointSlices.\n68: \tendpointSliceChangeMinSyncDelay = 1 * time.Second\n69: \n70: \t// defaultSyncBackOff is the default backoff period for syncService calls.\n71: \tdefaultSyncBackOff = 1 * time.Second\n72: \t// maxSyncBackOff is the max backoff period for syncService calls.\n73: \tmaxSyncBackOff = 1000 * time.Second\n74: \n75: \t// controllerName is a unique value used with LabelManagedBy to indicated\n76: \t// the component managing an EndpointSlice.\n77: \tcontrollerName = \"endpointslice-controller.k8s.io\"\n78: )\n79: \n80: // NewController creates and initializes a new Controller\n81: func NewController(ctx context.Context, podInformer coreinformers.PodInformer,\n82: \tserviceInformer coreinformers.ServiceInformer,\n83: \tnodeInformer coreinformers.NodeInformer,\n84: \tendpointSliceInformer discoveryinformers.EndpointSliceInformer,\n85: \tmaxEndpointsPerSlice int32,\n86: \tclient clientset.Interface,\n87: \tendpointUpdatesBatchPeriod time.Duration,\n88: ) *Controller {\n89: \tbroadcaster := record.NewBroadcaster(record.WithContext(ctx))\n90: \trecorder := broadcaster.NewRecorder(scheme.Scheme, v1.EventSource{Component: \"endpoint-slice-controller\"})\n91: \n92: \tendpointslicemetrics.RegisterMetrics()\n93: \n94: \tc := &Controller{\n95: \t\tclient: client,\n96: \t\t// This is similar to the DefaultControllerRateLimiter, just with a\n97: \t\t// significantly higher default backoff (1s vs 5ms). This controller\n98: \t\t// processes events that can require significant EndpointSlice changes,\n99: \t\t// such as an update to a Service or Deployment. A more significant\n100: \t\t// rate limit back off here helps ensure that the Controller does not\n101: \t\t// overwhelm the API Server.\n102: \t\tqueue: workqueue.NewNamedRateLimitingQueue(workqueue.NewMaxOfRateLimiter(\n103: \t\t\tworkqueue.NewItemExponentialFailureRateLimiter(defaultSyncBackOff, maxSyncBackOff),\n104: \t\t\t// 10 qps, 100 bucket size. This is only for retry speed and its\n105: \t\t\t// only the overall factor (not per item).\n106: \t\t\t&workqueue.BucketRateLimiter{Limiter: rate.NewLimiter(rate.Limit(10), 100)},\n107: \t\t), \"endpoint_slice\"),\n108: \t\tworkerLoopPeriod: time.Second,\n109: \t}\n110: \n111: \tserviceInformer.Informer().AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{\n112: \t\tAddFunc: c.onServiceUpdate,\n113: \t\tUpdateFunc: func(old, cur interface{}) {\n114: \t\t\tc.onServiceUpdate(cur)\n115: \t\t},\n116: \t\tDeleteFunc: c.onServiceDelete,\n117: \t})\n118: \tc.serviceLister = serviceInformer.Lister()\n119: \tc.servicesSynced = serviceInformer.Informer().HasSynced\n120: \n121: \tpodInformer.Informer().AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{\n122: \t\tAddFunc:    c.addPod,\n123: \t\tUpdateFunc: c.updatePod,\n124: \t\tDeleteFunc: c.deletePod,\n125: \t})\n126: \tc.podLister = podInformer.Lister()\n127: \tc.podsSynced = podInformer.Informer().HasSynced\n128: \n129: \tc.nodeLister = nodeInformer.Lister()\n130: \tc.nodesSynced = nodeInformer.Informer().HasSynced\n131: \n132: \tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n133: \tendpointSliceInformer.Informer().AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{\n134: \t\tAddFunc: c.onEndpointSliceAdd,\n135: \t\tUpdateFunc: func(oldObj, newObj interface{}) {\n136: \t\t\tc.onEndpointSliceUpdate(logger, oldObj, newObj)\n137: \t\t},\n138: \t\tDeleteFunc: c.onEndpointSliceDelete,\n139: \t})\n140: \n141: \tc.endpointSliceLister = endpointSliceInformer.Lister()\n142: \tc.endpointSlicesSynced = endpointSliceInformer.Informer().HasSynced\n143: \tc.endpointSliceTracker = endpointsliceutil.NewEndpointSliceTracker()\n144: \n145: \tc.maxEndpointsPerSlice = maxEndpointsPerSlice\n146: \n147: \tc.triggerTimeTracker = endpointsliceutil.NewTriggerTimeTracker()\n148: \n149: \tc.eventBroadcaster = broadcaster\n150: \tc.eventRecorder = recorder\n151: \n152: \tc.endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod = endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod\n153: \n154: \tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.TopologyAwareHints) {\n155: \t\tnodeInformer.Informer().AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{\n156: \t\t\tAddFunc: func(obj interface{}) {\n157: \t\t\t\tc.addNode(logger, obj)\n158: \t\t\t},\n159: \t\t\tUpdateFunc: func(oldObj, newObj interface{}) {\n160: \t\t\t\tc.updateNode(logger, oldObj, newObj)\n161: \t\t\t},\n162: \t\t\tDeleteFunc: func(obj interface{}) {\n163: \t\t\t\tc.deleteNode(logger, obj)\n164: \t\t\t},\n165: \t\t})\n166: \n167: \t\tc.topologyCache = topologycache.NewTopologyCache()\n168: \t}\n169: \n170: \tc.reconciler = endpointslicerec.NewReconciler(\n171: \t\tc.client,\n172: \t\tc.nodeLister,\n173: \t\tc.maxEndpointsPerSlice,\n174: \t\tc.endpointSliceTracker,\n175: \t\tc.topologyCache,\n176: \t\tc.eventRecorder,\n177: \t\tcontrollerName,\n178: \t\tendpointslicerec.WithTrafficDistributionEnabled(utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.ServiceTrafficDistribution)),\n179: \t)\n180: \n181: \treturn c\n182: }\n183: \n184: // Controller manages selector-based service endpoint slices\n185: type Controller struct {\n186: \tclient           clientset.Interface\n187: \teventBroadcaster record.EventBroadcaster\n188: \teventRecorder    record.EventRecorder\n189: \n190: \t// serviceLister is able to list/get services and is populated by the\n191: \t// shared informer passed to NewController\n192: \tserviceLister corelisters.ServiceLister\n193: \t// servicesSynced returns true if the service shared informer has been synced at least once.\n194: \t// Added as a member to the struct to allow injection for testing.\n195: \tservicesSynced cache.InformerSynced\n196: \n197: \t// podLister is able to list/get pods and is populated by the\n198: \t// shared informer passed to NewController\n199: \tpodLister corelisters.PodLister\n200: \t// podsSynced returns true if the pod shared informer has been synced at least once.\n201: \t// Added as a member to the struct to allow injection for testing.\n202: \tpodsSynced cache.InformerSynced\n203: \n204: \t// endpointSliceLister is able to list/get endpoint slices and is populated by the\n205: \t// shared informer passed to NewController\n206: \tendpointSliceLister discoverylisters.EndpointSliceLister\n207: \t// endpointSlicesSynced returns true if the endpoint slice shared informer has been synced at least once.\n208: \t// Added as a member to the struct to allow injection for testing.\n209: \tendpointSlicesSynced cache.InformerSynced\n210: \t// endpointSliceTracker tracks the list of EndpointSlices and associated\n211: \t// resource versions expected for each Service. It can help determine if a\n212: \t// cached EndpointSlice is out of date.\n213: \tendpointSliceTracker *endpointsliceutil.EndpointSliceTracker\n214: \n215: \t// nodeLister is able to list/get nodes and is populated by the\n216: \t// shared informer passed to NewController\n217: \tnodeLister corelisters.NodeLister\n218: \t// nodesSynced returns true if the node shared informer has been synced at least once.\n219: \t// Added as a member to the struct to allow injection for testing.\n220: \tnodesSynced cache.InformerSynced\n221: \n222: \t// reconciler is an util used to reconcile EndpointSlice changes.\n223: \treconciler *endpointslicerec.Reconciler\n224: \n225: \t// triggerTimeTracker is an util used to compute and export the\n226: \t// EndpointsLastChangeTriggerTime annotation.\n227: \ttriggerTimeTracker *endpointsliceutil.TriggerTimeTracker\n228: \n229: \t// Services that need to be updated. A channel is inappropriate here,\n230: \t// because it allows services with lots of pods to be serviced much\n231: \t// more often than services with few pods; it also would cause a\n232: \t// service that's inserted multiple times to be processed more than\n233: \t// necessary.\n234: \tqueue workqueue.RateLimitingInterface\n235: \n236: \t// maxEndpointsPerSlice references the maximum number of endpoints that\n237: \t// should be added to an EndpointSlice\n238: \tmaxEndpointsPerSlice int32\n239: \n240: \t// workerLoopPeriod is the time between worker runs. The workers\n241: \t// process the queue of service and pod changes\n242: \tworkerLoopPeriod time.Duration\n243: \n244: \t// endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod is an artificial delay added to all service syncs triggered by pod changes.\n245: \t// This can be used to reduce overall number of all endpoint slice updates.\n246: \tendpointUpdatesBatchPeriod time.Duration\n247: \n248: \t// topologyCache tracks the distribution of Nodes and endpoints across zones\n249: \t// to enable TopologyAwareHints.\n250: \ttopologyCache *topologycache.TopologyCache\n251: }\n252: \n253: // Run will not return until stopCh is closed.\n254: func (c *Controller) Run(ctx context.Context, workers int) {\n255: \tdefer utilruntime.HandleCrash()\n256: \n257: \t// Start events processing pipeline.\n258: \tc.eventBroadcaster.StartLogging(klog.Infof)\n259: \tc.eventBroadcaster.StartRecordingToSink(&v1core.EventSinkImpl{Interface: c.client.CoreV1().Events(\"\")})\n260: \tdefer c.eventBroadcaster.Shutdown()\n261: \n262: \tdefer c.queue.ShutDown()\n263: \n264: \tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\n265: \tlogger.Info(\"Starting endpoint slice controller\")\n266: \tdefer logger.Info(\"Shutting down endpoint slice controller\")\n267: \n268: \tif !cache.WaitForNamedCacheSync(\"endpoint_slice\", ctx.Done(), c.podsSynced, c.servicesSynced, c.endpointSlicesSynced, c.nodesSynced) {\n269: \t\treturn\n270: \t}\n271: \n272: \tlogger.V(2).Info(\"Starting worker threads\", \"total\", workers)\n273: \tfor i := 0; i < workers; i++ {\n274: \t\tgo wait.Until(func() { c.worker(logger) }, c.workerLoopPeriod, ctx.Done())\n275: \t}\n276: \n277: \t<-ctx.Done()\n278: }\n279: \n280: // worker runs a worker thread that just dequeues items, processes them, and\n281: // marks them done. You may run as many of these in parallel as you wish; the\n282: // workqueue guarantees that they will not end up processing the same service\n283: // at the same time\n284: func (c *Controller) worker(logger klog.Logger) {\n285: \tfor c.processNextWorkItem(logger) {\n286: \t}\n287: }\n288: \n289: func (c *Controller) processNextWorkItem(logger klog.Logger) bool {\n290: \tcKey, quit := c.queue.Get()\n291: \tif quit {\n292: \t\treturn false\n293: \t}\n294: \tdefer c.queue.Done(cKey)\n295: \n296: \terr := c.syncService(logger, cKey.(string))\n297: \tc.handleErr(logger, err, cKey)\n298: \n299: \treturn true\n300: }\n301: \n302: func (c *Controller) handleErr(logger klog.Logger, err error, key interface{}) {\n303: \ttrackSync(err)\n304: \n305: \tif err == nil {\n306: \t\tc.queue.Forget(key)\n307: \t\treturn\n308: \t}\n309: \n310: \tif c.queue.NumRequeues(key) < maxRetries {\n311: \t\tlogger.Info(\"Error syncing endpoint slices for service, retrying\", \"key\", key, \"err\", err)\n312: \t\tc.queue.AddRateLimited(key)\n313: \t\treturn\n314: \t}\n315: \n316: \tlogger.Info(\"Retry budget exceeded, dropping service out of the queue\", \"key\", key, \"err\", err)\n317: \tc.queue.Forget(key)\n318: \tutilruntime.HandleError(err)\n319: }\n320: \n321: func (c *Controller) syncService(logger klog.Logger, key string) error {\n322: \tstartTime := time.Now()\n323: \tdefer func() {\n324: \t\tlogger.V(4).Info(\"Finished syncing service endpoint slices\", \"key\", key, \"elapsedTime\", time.Since(startTime))\n325: \t}()\n326: \n327: \tnamespace, name, err := cache.SplitMetaNamespaceKey(key)\n328: \tif err != nil {\n329: \t\treturn err\n330: \t}\n331: \n332: \tservice, err := c.serviceLister.Services(namespace).Get(name)\n333: \tif err != nil {\n334: \t\tif !apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {\n335: \t\t\treturn err\n336: \t\t}\n337: \n338: \t\tc.triggerTimeTracker.DeleteService(namespace, name)\n339: \t\tc.reconciler.DeleteService(namespace, name)\n340: \t\tc.endpointSliceTracker.DeleteService(namespace, name)\n341: \t\t// The service has been deleted, return nil so that it won't be retried.\n342: \t\treturn nil\n343: \t}\n344: \n345: \tif service.Spec.Type == v1.ServiceTypeExternalName {\n346: \t\t// services with Type ExternalName receive no endpoints from this controller;\n347: \t\t// Ref: https://issues.k8s.io/105986\n348: \t\treturn nil\n349: \t}\n350: \n351: \tif service.Spec.Selector == nil {\n352: \t\t// services without a selector receive no endpoint slices from this controller;\n353: \t\t// these services will receive endpoint slices that are created out-of-band via the REST API.\n354: \t\treturn nil\n355: \t}\n356: \n357: \tlogger.V(5).Info(\"About to update endpoint slices for service\", \"key\", key)\n358: \n359: \tpodLabelSelector := labels.Set(service.Spec.Selector).AsSelectorPreValidated()\n360: \tpods, err := c.podLister.Pods(service.Namespace).List(podLabelSelector)\n361: \tif err != nil {\n362: \t\t// Since we're getting stuff from a local cache, it is basically\n363: \t\t// impossible to get this error.\n364: \t\tc.eventRecorder.Eventf(service, v1.EventTypeWarning, \"FailedToListPods\",\n365: \t\t\t\"Error listing Pods for Service %s/%s: %v\", service.Namespace, service.Name, err)\n366: \t\treturn err\n367: \t}\n368: \n369: \tesLabelSelector := labels.Set(map[string]string{\n370: \t\tdiscovery.LabelServiceName: service.Name,\n371: \t\tdiscovery.LabelManagedBy:   c.reconciler.GetControllerName(),\n372: \t}).AsSelectorPreValidated()\n373: \tendpointSlices, err := c.endpointSliceLister.EndpointSlices(service.Namespace).List(esLabelSelector)\n374: \n375: \tif err != nil {\n376: \t\t// Since we're getting stuff from a local cache, it is basically\n377: \t\t// impossible to get this error.\n378: \t\tc.eventRecorder.Eventf(service, v1.EventTypeWarning, \"FailedToListEndpointSlices\",\n379: \t\t\t\"Error listing Endpoint Slices for Service %s/%s: %v\", service.Namespace, service.Name, err)\n380: \t\treturn err\n381: \t}\n382: \n383: \t// Drop EndpointSlices that have been marked for deletion to prevent the controller from getting stuck.\n384: \tendpointSlices = dropEndpointSlicesPendingDeletion(endpointSlices)\n385: \n386: \tif c.endpointSliceTracker.StaleSlices(service, endpointSlices) {\n387: \t\treturn endpointslicepkg.NewStaleInformerCache(\"EndpointSlice informer cache is out of date\")\n388: \t}\n389: \n390: \t// We call ComputeEndpointLastChangeTriggerTime here to make sure that the\n391: \t// state of the trigger time tracker gets updated even if the sync turns out\n392: \t// to be no-op and we don't update the EndpointSlice objects.\n393: \tlastChangeTriggerTime := c.triggerTimeTracker.\n394: \t\tComputeEndpointLastChangeTriggerTime(namespace, service, pods)\n395: \n396: \terr = c.reconciler.Reconcile(logger, service, pods, endpointSlices, lastChangeTriggerTime)\n397: \tif err != nil {\n398: \t\tc.eventRecorder.Eventf(service, v1.EventTypeWarning, \"FailedToUpdateEndpointSlices\",\n399: \t\t\t\"Error updating Endpoint Slices for Service %s/%s: %v\", service.Namespace, service.Name, err)\n400: \t\treturn err\n401: \t}\n402: \n403: \treturn nil\n404: }\n405: \n406: // onServiceUpdate updates the Service Selector in the cache and queues the Service for processing.\n407: func (c *Controller) onServiceUpdate(obj interface{}) {\n408: \tkey, err := controller.KeyFunc(obj)\n409: \tif err != nil {\n410: \t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"Couldn't get key for object %+v: %v\", obj, err))\n411: \t\treturn\n412: \t}\n413: \n414: \tc.queue.Add(key)\n415: }\n416: \n417: // onServiceDelete removes the Service Selector from the cache and queues the Service for processing.\n418: func (c *Controller) onServiceDelete(obj interface{}) {\n419: \tkey, err := controller.KeyFunc(obj)\n420: \tif err != nil {\n421: \t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"Couldn't get key for object %+v: %v\", obj, err))\n422: \t\treturn\n423: \t}\n424: \n425: \tc.queue.Add(key)\n426: }\n427: \n428: // onEndpointSliceAdd queues a sync for the relevant Service for a sync if the\n429: // EndpointSlice resource version does not match the expected version in the\n430: // endpointSliceTracker.\n431: func (c *Controller) onEndpointSliceAdd(obj interface{}) {\n432: \tendpointSlice := obj.(*discovery.EndpointSlice)\n433: \tif endpointSlice == nil {\n434: \t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"Invalid EndpointSlice provided to onEndpointSliceAdd()\"))\n435: \t\treturn\n436: \t}\n437: \tif c.reconciler.ManagedByController(endpointSlice) && c.endpointSliceTracker.ShouldSync(endpointSlice) {\n438: \t\tc.queueServiceForEndpointSlice(endpointSlice)\n439: \t}\n440: }\n441: \n442: // onEndpointSliceUpdate queues a sync for the relevant Service for a sync if\n443: // the EndpointSlice resource version does not match the expected version in the\n444: // endpointSliceTracker or the managed-by value of the EndpointSlice has changed\n445: // from or to this controller.\n446: func (c *Controller) onEndpointSliceUpdate(logger klog.Logger, prevObj, obj interface{}) {\n447: \tprevEndpointSlice := prevObj.(*discovery.EndpointSlice)\n448: \tendpointSlice := obj.(*discovery.EndpointSlice)\n449: \tif endpointSlice == nil || prevEndpointSlice == nil {\n450: \t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"Invalid EndpointSlice provided to onEndpointSliceUpdate()\"))\n451: \t\treturn\n452: \t}\n453: \t// EndpointSlice generation does not change when labels change. Although the\n454: \t// controller will never change LabelServiceName, users might. This check\n455: \t// ensures that we handle changes to this label.\n456: \tsvcName := endpointSlice.Labels[discovery.LabelServiceName]\n457: \tprevSvcName := prevEndpointSlice.Labels[discovery.LabelServiceName]\n458: \tif svcName != prevSvcName {\n459: \t\tlogger.Info(\"label changed\", \"label\", discovery.LabelServiceName, \"oldService\", prevSvcName, \"newService\", svcName, \"endpointslice\", klog.KObj(endpointSlice))\n460: \t\tc.queueServiceForEndpointSlice(endpointSlice)\n461: \t\tc.queueServiceForEndpointSlice(prevEndpointSlice)\n462: \t\treturn\n463: \t}\n464: \tif c.reconciler.ManagedByChanged(prevEndpointSlice, endpointSlice) || (c.reconciler.ManagedByController(endpointSlice) && c.endpointSliceTracker.ShouldSync(endpointSlice)) {\n465: \t\tc.queueServiceForEndpointSlice(endpointSlice)\n466: \t}\n467: }\n468: \n469: // onEndpointSliceDelete queues a sync for the relevant Service for a sync if the\n470: // EndpointSlice resource version does not match the expected version in the\n471: // endpointSliceTracker.\n472: func (c *Controller) onEndpointSliceDelete(obj interface{}) {\n473: \tendpointSlice := getEndpointSliceFromDeleteAction(obj)\n474: \tif endpointSlice != nil && c.reconciler.ManagedByController(endpointSlice) && c.endpointSliceTracker.Has(endpointSlice) {\n475: \t\t// This returns false if we didn't expect the EndpointSlice to be\n476: \t\t// deleted. If that is the case, we queue the Service for another sync.\n477: \t\tif !c.endpointSliceTracker.HandleDeletion(endpointSlice) {\n478: \t\t\tc.queueServiceForEndpointSlice(endpointSlice)\n479: \t\t}\n480: \t}\n481: }\n482: \n483: // queueServiceForEndpointSlice attempts to queue the corresponding Service for\n484: // the provided EndpointSlice.\n485: func (c *Controller) queueServiceForEndpointSlice(endpointSlice *discovery.EndpointSlice) {\n486: \tkey, err := endpointslicerec.ServiceControllerKey(endpointSlice)\n487: \tif err != nil {\n488: \t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"Couldn't get key for EndpointSlice %+v: %v\", endpointSlice, err))\n489: \t\treturn\n490: \t}\n491: \n492: \t// queue after the max of endpointSliceChangeMinSyncDelay and\n493: \t// endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod.\n494: \tdelay := endpointSliceChangeMinSyncDelay\n495: \tif c.endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod > delay {\n496: \t\tdelay = c.endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod\n497: \t}\n498: \tc.queue.AddAfter(key, delay)\n499: }\n500: \n501: func (c *Controller) addPod(obj interface{}) {\n502: \tpod := obj.(*v1.Pod)\n503: \tservices, err := endpointsliceutil.GetPodServiceMemberships(c.serviceLister, pod)\n504: \tif err != nil {\n505: \t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"Unable to get pod %s/%s's service memberships: %v\", pod.Namespace, pod.Name, err))\n506: \t\treturn\n507: \t}\n508: \tfor key := range services {\n509: \t\tc.queue.AddAfter(key, c.endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod)\n510: \t}\n511: }\n512: \n513: func (c *Controller) updatePod(old, cur interface{}) {\n514: \tservices := endpointsliceutil.GetServicesToUpdateOnPodChange(c.serviceLister, old, cur)\n515: \tfor key := range services {\n516: \t\tc.queue.AddAfter(key, c.endpointUpdatesBatchPeriod)\n517: \t}\n518: }\n519: \n520: // When a pod is deleted, enqueue the services the pod used to be a member of\n521: // obj could be an *v1.Pod, or a DeletionFinalStateUnknown marker item.\n522: func (c *Controller) deletePod(obj interface{}) {\n523: \tpod := endpointsliceutil.GetPodFromDeleteAction(obj)\n524: \tif pod != nil {\n525: \t\tc.addPod(pod)\n526: \t}\n527: }\n528: \n529: func (c *Controller) addNode(logger klog.Logger, obj interface{}) {\n530: \tc.checkNodeTopologyDistribution(logger)\n531: }\n532: \n533: func (c *Controller) updateNode(logger klog.Logger, old, cur interface{}) {\n534: \toldNode := old.(*v1.Node)\n535: \tcurNode := cur.(*v1.Node)\n536: \n537: \t// LabelTopologyZone may be added by cloud provider asynchronously after the Node is created.\n538: \t// The topology cache should be updated in this case.\n539: \tif isNodeReady(oldNode) != isNodeReady(curNode) ||\n540: \t\toldNode.Labels[v1.LabelTopologyZone] != curNode.Labels[v1.LabelTopologyZone] {\n541: \t\tc.checkNodeTopologyDistribution(logger)\n542: \t}\n543: }\n544: \n545: func (c *Controller) deleteNode(logger klog.Logger, obj interface{}) {\n546: \tc.checkNodeTopologyDistribution(logger)\n547: }\n548: \n549: // checkNodeTopologyDistribution updates Nodes in the topology cache and then\n550: // queues any Services that are past the threshold.\n551: func (c *Controller) checkNodeTopologyDistribution(logger klog.Logger) {\n552: \tif c.topologyCache == nil {\n553: \t\treturn\n554: \t}\n555: \tnodes, err := c.nodeLister.List(labels.Everything())\n556: \tif err != nil {\n557: \t\tlogger.Error(err, \"Error listing Nodes\")\n558: \t\treturn\n559: \t}\n560: \tc.topologyCache.SetNodes(logger, nodes)\n561: \tserviceKeys := c.topologyCache.GetOverloadedServices()\n562: \tfor _, serviceKey := range serviceKeys {\n563: \t\tlogger.V(2).Info(\"Queuing Service after Node change due to overloading\", \"key\", serviceKey)\n564: \t\tc.queue.Add(serviceKey)\n565: \t}\n566: }\n567: \n568: // trackSync increments the EndpointSliceSyncs metric with the result of a sync.\n569: func trackSync(err error) {\n570: \tmetricLabel := \"success\"\n571: \tif err != nil {\n572: \t\tif endpointslicepkg.IsStaleInformerCacheErr(err) {\n573: \t\t\tmetricLabel = \"stale\"\n574: \t\t} else {\n575: \t\t\tmetricLabel = \"error\"\n576: \t\t}\n577: \t}\n578: \tendpointslicemetrics.EndpointSliceSyncs.WithLabelValues(metricLabel).Inc()\n579: }\n580: \n581: func dropEndpointSlicesPendingDeletion(endpointSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice) []*discovery.EndpointSlice {\n582: \tn := 0\n583: \tfor _, endpointSlice := range endpointSlices {\n584: \t\tif endpointSlice.DeletionTimestamp == nil {\n585: \t\t\tendpointSlices[n] = endpointSlice\n586: \t\t\tn++\n587: \t\t}\n588: \t}\n589: \treturn endpointSlices[:n]\n590: }\n591: \n592: // getEndpointSliceFromDeleteAction parses an EndpointSlice from a delete action.\n593: func getEndpointSliceFromDeleteAction(obj interface{}) *discovery.EndpointSlice {\n594: \tif endpointSlice, ok := obj.(*discovery.EndpointSlice); ok {\n595: \t\t// Enqueue all the services that the pod used to be a member of.\n596: \t\t// This is the same thing we do when we add a pod.\n597: \t\treturn endpointSlice\n598: \t}\n599: \t// If we reached here it means the pod was deleted but its final state is unrecorded.\n600: \ttombstone, ok := obj.(cache.DeletedFinalStateUnknown)\n601: \tif !ok {\n602: \t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"Couldn't get object from tombstone %#v\", obj))\n603: \t\treturn nil\n604: \t}\n605: \tendpointSlice, ok := tombstone.Obj.(*discovery.EndpointSlice)\n606: \tif !ok {\n607: \t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"Tombstone contained object that is not a EndpointSlice: %#v\", obj))\n608: \t\treturn nil\n609: \t}\n610: \treturn endpointSlice\n611: }\n612: \n613: // isNodeReady returns true if a node is ready; false otherwise.\n614: func isNodeReady(node *v1.Node) bool {\n615: \tfor _, c := range node.Status.Conditions {\n616: \t\tif c.Type == v1.NodeReady {\n617: \t\t\treturn c.Status == v1.ConditionTrue\n618: \t\t}\n619: \t}\n620: \treturn false\n621: }\n622: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/pkg/controller/endpointslice/endpointslice_controller.go"
  }
}

  }
}

\\n21: // DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc returns the filter function that can\\n22: // filter out the node taints that reject scheduling Pod on a Node.\\n23: func DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc() func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\\n24: \\treturn func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\\n25: \\t\\t// PodToleratesNodeTaints is only interested in NoSchedule and NoExecute taints.\\n26: \\t\\treturn t.Effect ==       {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "418: \n419: func toleratesTaintsWithNoScheduleNoExecuteEffects(taints []v1.Taint, tolerations []v1.Toleration) bool {\n420: \tfilteredTaints := []v1.Taint{}\n421: \tfor _, taint := range taints {\n422: \t\tif taint.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute || taint.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule {\n423: \t\t\tfilteredTaints = append(filteredTaints, taint)\n424: \t\t}\n425: ",
            "endLine": 425,
            "startLine": 418
          },
          {
            "content": "415: \n416: \treturn toleratesTaintsWithNoScheduleNoExecuteEffects(n.Spec.Taints, nil)\n417: }\n418: ",
            "endLine": 418,
            "startLine": 415
          },
          {
            "content": "59: var (\n60: \t// unreachableTaintTemplate is the taint for when a node becomes unreachable.\n61: \t// Copied from pkg/controller/nodelifecycle to avoid pulling extra dependencies\n62: \tunreachableTaintTemplate = &v1.Taint{\n63: \t\tKey:    v1.TaintNodeUnreachable,\n64: \t\tEffect: v1.TaintEffectNoExecute,\n65: \t}\n66: ",
            "endLine": 66,
            "startLine": 59
          }
        ],

        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "216: \t\tfor i := 0; i < 10; i++ {\n217: \t\t\ttestTaint := getRandomTaint()\n218: \t\t\ttolerableTaints = append(tolerableTaints, testTaint)\n219: \t\t\ttolerations = append(tolerations, v1.Toleration{Key: testTaint.Key, Value: testTaint.Value, Effect: testTaint.Effect})\n220: \t\t}\n221: \t\t// Generate 10 intolerable taints for each of the remaining nodes\n222: \t\tintolerableTaints := make(map[string][]v1.Taint)\n223: \t\tfor i := 1; i < len(nodeList.Items); i++ {\n224: \t\t\tnodeTaints := make([]v1.Taint, 0)\n225: \t\t\tfor i := 0; i < 10; i++ {\n226: \t\t\t\tnodeTaints = append(nodeTaints, getRandomTaint())\n227: \t\t\t}\n228: \t\t\tintolerableTaints[nodeList.Items[i].Name] = nodeTaints\n229: \t\t}\n230: ",

            "content": "549: \n550: func getRandomTaint() v1.Taint {\n551: \treturn v1.Taint{\n552: \t\tKey:    fmt.Sprintf(\"kubernetes.io/e2e-scheduling-priorities-%s\", string(uuid.NewUUID()[:23])),\n553: \t\tValue:  fmt.Sprintf(\"testing-taint-value-%s\", string(uuid.NewUUID())),\n554: \t\tEffect: v1.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule,\n555: \t}\n556: ",
            "endLine": 556,
            "startLine": 549
          },
          {
            "content": "557: \n558: func addTaintToNode(ctx context.Context, cs clientset.Interface, nodeName string, testTaint v1.Taint) {\n559: \te2enode.AddOrUpdateTaintOnNode(ctx, cs, nodeName, testTaint)\n560: \te2enode.ExpectNodeHasTaint(ctx, cs, nodeName, &testTaint)\n561: }\n562: ",
            "endLine": 562,

        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "20: \n21: // DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc returns the filter function that can\n22: // filter out the node taints that reject scheduling Pod on a Node.\n23: func DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc() func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\n24: \treturn func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\n25: \t\t// PodToleratesNodeTaints is only interested in NoSchedule and NoExecute taints.\n26: \t\treturn t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute\n27: \t}\n28: ",
            "endLine": 28,
            "startLine": 20
          }
        ],

            "content": "153:   ### kubectl uncordon update with --dry-run is a no-op\n154:   # Pre-condition: node is already schedulable\n155:   kube::test::get_object_assert \"nodes 127.0.0.1\" \"{{.spec.unschedulable}}\" '<no value>'\n156:   response=$(kubectl uncordon \"127.0.0.1\" --dry-run=client)\n157:   kube::test::if_has_string \"${response}\" 'already uncordoned'\n158:   response=$(kubectl uncordon \"127.0.0.1\" --dry-run=server)\n159:   kube::test::if_has_string \"${response}\" 'already uncordoned'\n160:   # Post-condition: node is still schedulable\n161:   kube::test::get_object_assert \"nodes 127.0.0.1\" \"{{.spec.unschedulable}}\" '<no value>'\n162: \n163: ",

19. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 3550,
  "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
  "startLine": 3500
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"3500: \\t// TimeAdded represents the time at which the taint was added.\\n3501: \\t// It is only written for NoExecute taints.\\n3502: \\t// +optional\\n3503: \\tTimeAdded *metav1.Time `json:\\\"timeAdded,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=timeAdded\\\"`\\n3504: }\\n3505: \\n3506: // +enum\\n3507: type TaintEffect string\\n3508: \\n3509: const (\\n3510: \\t// Do not allow new pods to schedule onto the node unless they tolerate the taint,\\n3511: \\t// but allow all pods submitted to Kubelet without going through the scheduler\\n3512: \\t// to start, and allow all already-running pods to continue running.\\n3513: \\t// Enforced by the scheduler.\\n3514: \\tTaintEffectNoSchedule TaintEffect = \\\"NoSchedule\\\"\\n3515: \\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but the scheduler tries not to schedule\\n3516: \\t// new pods onto the node, rather than prohibiting new pods from scheduling\\n3517: \\t// onto the node entirely. Enforced by the scheduler.\\n3518: \\tTaintEffectPreferNoSchedule TaintEffect = \\\"PreferNoSchedule\\\"\\n3519: \\t// NOT YET IMPLEMENTED. TODO: Uncomment field once it is implemented.\\n3520: \\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally do not allow pods submitted to\\n3521: \\t// Kubelet without going through the scheduler to start.\\n3522: \\t// Enforced by Kubelet and the scheduler.\\n3523: \\t// TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit TaintEffect = \\\"NoScheduleNoAdmit\\\"\\n3524: \\n3525: \\t// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\\n3526: \\t// Currently enforced by NodeController.\\n3527: \\tTaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \\\"NoExecute\\\"\\n3528: )\\n3529: \\n3530: // The pod this Toleration is attached to tolerates any taint that matches\\n3531: // the triple <key,value,effect> using the matching operator <operator>.\\n3532: type Toleration struct {\\n3533: \\t// Key is the taint key that the toleration applies to. Empty means match all taint keys.\\n3534: \\t// If the key is empty, operator must be Exists; this combination means to match all values and all keys.\\n3535: \\t// +optional\\n3536: \\tKey string `json:\\\"key,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,1,opt,name=key\\\"`\\n3537: \\t// Operator represents a key's relationship to the value.\\n3538: \\t// Valid operators are Exists and Equal. Defaults to Equal.\\n3539: \\t// Exists is equivalent to wildcard for value, so that a pod can\\n3540: \\t// tolerate all taints of a particular category.\\n3541: \\t// +optional\\n3542: \\tOperator TolerationOperator `json:\\\"operator,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,2,opt,name=operator,casttype=TolerationOperator\\\"`\\n3543: \\t// Value is the taint value the toleration matches to.\\n3544: \\t// If the operator is Exists, the value should be empty, otherwise just a regular string.\\n3545: \\t// +optional\\n3546: \\tValue string `json:\\\"value,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,3,opt,name=value\\\"`\\n3547: \\t// Effect indicates the taint effect to match. Empty means match all taint effects.\\n3548: \\t// When specified, allowed values are NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule and NoExecute.\\n3549: \\t// +optional\\n3550: \\tEffect TaintEffect `json:\\\"effect,omitempty\\\" protobuf:\\\"bytes,4,opt,name=effect,casttype=TaintEffect\\\"`\\n3551: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go%3FL3500-3550\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "3500: \t// TimeAdded represents the time at which the taint was added.\n3501: \t// It is only written for NoExecute taints.\n3502: \t// +optional\n3503: \tTimeAdded *metav1.Time `json:\"timeAdded,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=timeAdded\"`\n3504: }\n3505: \n3506: // +enum\n3507: type TaintEffect string\n3508: \n3509: const (\n3510: \t// Do not allow new pods to schedule onto the node unless they tolerate the taint,\n3511: \t// but allow all pods submitted to Kubelet without going through the scheduler\n3512: \t// to start, and allow all already-running pods to continue running.\n3513: \t// Enforced by the scheduler.\n3514: \tTaintEffectNoSchedule TaintEffect = \"NoSchedule\"\n3515: \t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but the scheduler tries not to schedule\n3516: \t// new pods onto the node, rather than prohibiting new pods from scheduling\n3517: \t// onto the node entirely. Enforced by the scheduler.\n3518: \tTaintEffectPreferNoSchedule TaintEffect = \"PreferNoSchedule\"\n3519: \t// NOT YET IMPLEMENTED. TODO: Uncomment field once it is implemented.\n3520: \t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally do not allow pods submitted to\n3521: \t// Kubelet without going through the scheduler to start.\n3522: \t// Enforced by Kubelet and the scheduler.\n3523: \t// TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoAdmit\"\n3524: \n3525: \t// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\n3526: \t// Currently enforced by NodeController.\n3527: \tTaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \"NoExecute\"\n3528: )\n3529: \n3530: // The pod this Toleration is attached to tolerates any taint that matches\n3531: // the triple <key,value,effect> using the matching operator <operator>.\n3532: type Toleration struct {\n3533: \t// Key is the taint key that the toleration applies to. Empty means match all taint keys.\n3534: \t// If the key is empty, operator must be Exists; this combination means to match all values and all keys.\n3535: \t// +optional\n3536: \tKey string `json:\"key,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=key\"`\n3537: \t// Operator represents a key's relationship to the value.\n3538: \t// Valid operators are Exists and Equal. Defaults to Equal.\n3539: \t// Exists is equivalent to wildcard for value, so that a pod can\n3540: \t// tolerate all taints of a particular category.\n3541: \t// +optional\n3542: \tOperator TolerationOperator `json:\"operator,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=operator,casttype=TolerationOperator\"`\n3543: \t// Value is the taint value the toleration matches to.\n3544: \t// If the operator is Exists, the value should be empty, otherwise just a regular string.\n3545: \t// +optional\n3546: \tValue string `json:\"value,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=value\"`\n3547: \t// Effect indicates the taint effect to match. Empty means match all taint effects.\n3548: \t// When specified, allowed values are NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule and NoExecute.\n3549: \t// +optional\n3550: \tEffect TaintEffect `json:\"effect,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=effect,casttype=TaintEffect\"`\n3551: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go%3FL3500-3550"
  }
}

  "content": "{\"blocks\":[{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"66: \\n67: \\t// notReadyTaintTemplate is the taint for when a node is not ready for executing pods.\\n68: \\t// Copied from pkg/controller/nodelifecycle to avoid pulling extra dependencies\\n69: \\tnotReadyTaintTemplate = &v1.Taint{\\n70: \\t\\tKey:    v1.TaintNodeNotReady,\\n71: \\t\\tEffect: v1.TaintEffectNoExecute,\\n72: \",\"endLine\":72,\"startLine\":66},{\"content\":\"455: \\n456: // IsNodeReady returns true if:\\n457: // 1) it's Ready condition is set to true\\n458: // 2) doesn't have NetworkUnavailable condition set to true\\n459: func IsNodeReady(node *v1.Node) bool {\\n460: \\tnodeReady := IsConditionSetAsExpected(node, v1.NodeReady, true)\\n461: \\tnetworkReady := isConditionUnset(node, v1.NodeNetworkUnavailable) ||\\n462: \\t\\tIsConditionSetAsExpectedSilent(node, v1.NodeNetworkUnavailable, false)\\n463: \\treturn nodeReady && networkReady\\n464: }\\n465: \",\"endLine\":465,\"startLine\":455},{\"content\":\"204: \\n205: // TotalReady returns number of ready schedulable Nodes.\\n206: func TotalReady(ctx context.Context, c clientset.Interface) (int, error) {\\n207: \\tnodes, err := waitListSchedulableNodes(ctx, c)\\n208: \",\"endLine\":208,\"startLine\":204}],\"file\":\"test/e2e/framework/node/resource.go\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/test/e2e/framework/node/resource.go\"}]}",

\\t\\t\\t}\\n195: \\t\\t\\t// If the Node specified by the Pod doesn't exist we want to requeue the Service so we\\n196: \\t\\t\\t// retry later, but also update the EndpointSlice without the problematic Pod.\\n197: \\t\\t\\t// Theoretically, the pod Garbage Collector will remove the Pod, but we want to avoid\\n198: \\t\\t\\t// situations where a reference from a Pod to a missing node can leave the EndpointSlice\\n199: \\t\\t\\t// stuck forever.\\n200: \\t\\t\\t// On the other side, if the service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses is set we just add the\\n201: \\t\\t\\t// Pod, since the
