\\n1521: // When a Pod references a Node that is not present in the informer cache the\\n1522: // EndpointSlices will continue to allow create, updates and deletes through.\\n1523: // The Pod with the missing reference will be published or retried depending of\\n1524: // the service.spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses.\\n1525: // The test considers two Pods on different nodes, one of the Nodes is not present.\\n1526: func TestReconcilerPodMissingNode(t *testing.T) {\\n1527: \\tnamespace := \\\"test\\\"\\n1528: \",\"endLine\":1528,\"startLine\":1520},{\"content\":\"691: \\n692: // when a Service has PublishNotReadyAddresses set to true, corresponding\\n693: // Endpoints should be considered ready, even if the backing Pod is not.\\n694: func TestReconcile1EndpointSlicePublishNotReadyAddresses(t *testing.T) {\\n695: \\tclient := newClientset()\\n696: \",\"endLine\":696,\"startLine\":691},{\"content\":\"774: \\t// have approximately 1/4 in first slice\\n775: \\tendpointSlice1 := newEmptyEndpointSlice(1, namespace, endpointMeta, svc)\\n776: \\tfor i := 1; i < len(pods)-4; i += 4 {\\n777: \\t\\tendpointSlice1.Endpoints = append(endpointSlice1.Endpoints, podToEndpoint(pods[i], &corev1.Node{}, &svc, discovery.AddressTypeIPv4))\\n778: \\t}\\n779: \",\"endLine\":779,\"startLine\":774}],\"file\":\"staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler_test.go\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler_test.go\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"2208: \\n2209: ### Workloads that saturate nodes with pods may see pods that fail due to node admission\\n2210: \\n2211: 1.22 addressed a long-standing issue in the Kubelet where terminating pods were [vulnerable to race conditions](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/102344) leading to early shutdown, resource leaks, or long delays in actually completing pod shutd ... [line truncated]\\n2212: \\n2213: \",\"endLine\":2213,\"startLine\":2208},{\"content\":\"283:   - [Known Issues](#known-issues)\\n284:     - [CPU and Memory manager are not working correctly for Guaranteed Pods with multiple containers](#cpu-and-memory-manager-are-not-working-correctly-for-guaranteed-pods-with-multiple-containers)\\n285:     - [<code>CSIMigrationvSphere</code> feature gate has not migrated to new CRD APIs](#csimigrationvsphere-feature-gate-has-not-migrated-to-new-crd-apis)\\n286:     - [Workloads that saturate nodes with pods may see pods that fail due to node admission](#workloads-that-saturate-nodes-with-pods-may-see-pods-that-fail-due-to-node-admission)\\n287:     - [Etcd v3.5.[0-2] data corruption](#etcd-v350-2-data-corruption)\\n288: \",\"endLine\":288,\"startLine\":283},{\"content\":\"1158: \\n1159: - Bug Fix: Kube-proxy dropped endpointSlice's local endpoints when upgrading from 1.20 to 1.22 ([#110245](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110245), [@xh4n3](https://github.com/xh4n3)) [SIG Network]\\n1160: - EndpointSlices marked for deletion are now ignored during reconciliation. ([#110482](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110482), [@aryan9600](https://github.com/aryan9600)) [SIG Apps and Network]\\n1161: - Fixed a kubelet issue that could result in invalid pod status updates to be sent to the api-server where pods would be reported in a terminal phase but also report a ready condition of true in some cases. ([#110481](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernet ... [line truncated]\\n1162: - Pods will now post their readiness during termination. ([#110418](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110418), [@aojea](https://github.com/aojea)) [SIG Network, Node and Testing]\\n1163: ... [chunk truncated]\",\"endLine\":1165,\"startLine\":1158}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.22.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.22.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"3364: \\n3365: - EndpointSlices with Pod referencing Nodes that doesn't exist couldn't be created or updated.\\n3366:   The behavior on the EndpointSlice controller has been modified to update the EndpointSlice without the Pods that reference non-existing Nodes, and keep retrying until all Pods reference existing Nodes.\\n3367:   However, if service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses is set, all the Pods are published without retrying.\\n3368:   Fixed EndpointSlices metrics to reflect correctly the number of desired EndpointSlices when no endpoints are present. ([#110639](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110639), [@aojea](https://github.com/aojea)) [SIG Apps and Network]\\n3369: \\n3370: \",\"endLine\":3370,\"startLine\":3364},{\"content\":\"2466: \\n2467: - EndpointSlices with Pod referencing Nodes that don't exist couldn't be created or updated. The behavior on the EndpointSlice controller has been modified to update the EndpointSlice without the Pods that reference non-existing Nodes and keep retrying unt ... [line truncated]\\n2468: - Optimization of kubectl Chinese translation ([#110538](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110538), [@hwdef](https://github.com/hwdef)) [SIG CLI]\\n2469: \",\"endLine\":2469,\"startLine\":2466},{\"content\":\"3584: - The kube-proxy `sync_proxy_rules_no_endpoints_total` metric now only counts local-traffic-policy services which have remote endpoints but not local endpoints. ([#109782](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/109782), [@danwinship](https://github. ... [line truncated]\\n3585: - The pod phase lifecycle guarantees that terminal Pods, those whose states are Unready or Succeeded, can not regress and will have all container stopped. Hence, terminal Pods will never be reachable and should not publish their IP addresses on the Endpoin ... [line truncated]\\n3586: - Upgrade Azure/go-autorest/autorest to v0.11.27 ([#110371](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110371), [@andyzhangx](https://github.com/andyzhangx)) [SIG Cloud Provider]\\n3587: \",\"endLine\":3587,\"startLine\":3584}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"2969: \\n2970: - Add gRPC probe to Pod.Spec.Container.{Liveness,Readiness,Startup}Probe (#106463, @SergeyKanzhelev) [SIG API Machinery, Apps, CLI, Node and Testing]\\n2971: - Adds a feature gate StatefulSetAutoDeletePVC, which allows PVCs automatically created for StatefulSet pods to be automatically deleted. (#99728, @mattcary) [SIG API Machinery, Apps, Auth and Testing]\\n2972: - Performs strict server side schema validation requests via the `fieldValidation=[Strict,Warn,Ignore]` query parameter. (#105916, @kevindelgado) [SIG API Machinery, Apps, Auth, Cloud Provider and Testing]\\n2973: - Support pod priority based node graceful shutdown (#102915, @wzshiming) [SIG Node and Testing]\\n2974: \\n2975: \",\"endLine\":2975,\"startLine\":2969},{\"content\":\"1493: \\n1494: - EndpointSlices marked for deletion are now ignored during reconciliation. ([#110483](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110483), [@aryan9600](https://github.com/aryan9600)) [SIG Apps and Network]\\n1495: - Fixed a kubelet issue that could result in invalid pod status updates to be sent to the api-server where pods would be reported in a terminal phase but also report a ready condition of true in some cases. ([#110480](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernet ... [line truncated]\\n1496: - Pods will now post their readiness during termination. ([#110417](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110417), [@aojea](https://github.com/aojea)) [SIG Network, Node and Testing]\\n1497: - The pod phase lifecycle guarantees that terminal Pods, those whose states are Unready or Succeeded, can not regress and will have all container stopped. Hence, terminal Pods will never be reachable and should not publish their IP addresses on the Endpoin ... [line truncated]\\n1498: \\n1499: \",\"endLine\":1499,\"startLine\":1493},{\"content\":\"3073: - KubeSchedulerConfiguration provides a new field `MultiPoint` which will register a plugin for all valid extension points (#105611, @damemi) [SIG Scheduling and Testing]\\n3074: - Kubelet should reject pods whose OS doesn't match the node's OS label. (#105292, @ravisantoshgudimetla) [SIG Apps and Node]\\n3075: - The CSIVolumeFSGroupPolicy feature has moved from beta to GA. (#105940, @dobsonj) [SIG Storage]\\n3076: - The Kubelet's `--register-with-taints` option is now available via the Kubelet config file field registerWithTaints (#105437, @cmssczy) [SIG Node and Scalability]\\n3077: - Validation rules for Custom Resource Definitions can be written in the [CEL expression language](https://github.com/google/cel-spec) using the `x-kubernetes-validations` extension in OpenAPIv3 schemas (alpha). This is gated by the alpha \\\"CustomResourceVa ... [line truncated]\\n3078: \\n3079: \",\"endLine\":3079,\"startLine\":3073}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.23.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.23.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"1586: - kubeadm now deletes the bootstrap-kubelet.conf file after TLS bootstrap\\n1587:   User relying on bootstrap-kubelet.conf should switch to kubelet.conf that contains node credentials ([#80676](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/80676), [@fabriziopandini](https://github.com/fabriziopandini))\\n1588: - Node labels `beta.kubernetes.io/metadata-proxy-ready`, `beta.kubernetes.io/metadata-proxy-ready` and `beta.kubernetes.io/kube-proxy-ds-ready` are no longer added on new nodes.\\n1589:   - ip-mask-agent addon starts to use the label `node.kubernetes.io/masq-agent-ds-ready` instead of `beta.kubernetes.io/masq-agent-ds-ready` as its node selector.\\n1590:   - kube-proxy addon starts to use the label `node.kubernetes.io/kube-proxy-ds-ready` instead of `beta.kubernetes.io/kube-proxy-ds-ready` as its node selector.\\n1591:   - metadata-proxy addon starts to use the label `cloud.google.com/metadata-proxy-ready` instead of `beta.kubernetes.io/metadata-proxy-ready` as its node selector.\\n1592: \\n1593: \",\"endLine\":1593,\"startLine\":1586},{\"content\":\"2891: * Migrate scheduler to use v1beta1 Event API. action required: any tool targeting scheduler events needs to use v1beta1 Event API ([#78447](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/78447), [@yastij](https://github.com/yastij))\\n2892: * scheduler.alpha.kubernetes.io/critical-pod annotation is removed. Pod priority (spec.priorityClassName) should be used instead to mark pods as critical. Action required! ([#79554](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/79554), [@draveness](https:/ ... [line truncated]\\n2893: * hyperkube: the `--make-symlinks` flag, deprecated in v1.14, has been removed. ([#80017](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/80017), [@Pothulapati](https://github.com/Pothulapati))\\n2894: * Node labels `beta.kubernetes.io/metadata-proxy-ready`, `beta.kubernetes.io/metadata-proxy-ready` and `beta.kubernetes.io/kube-proxy-ds-ready` are no longer added on new nodes. ([#79305](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/79305), [@paivagustavo ... [line truncated]\\n2895: ... [chunk truncated]\",\"endLine\":2900,\"startLine\":2891},{\"content\":\"1936: \\n1937: ### Node\\n1938: \\n1939: \",\"endLine\":1939,\"startLine\":1936}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.16.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.16.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"2470: - Docker runtime support using dockershim in the kubelet is now completely removed in 1.24. The kubelet used to have a module called dockershim, which implements CRI support for Docker, and it has seen maintenance issues in the Kubernetes community. From 1 ... [line truncated]\\n2471: - Fixed bug with leads to Node goes `Not-ready` state when credentials for vCenter stored in a secret and Zones feature is in use. Zone labels setup moved to KCM component, kubelet skips this step during startup in such case. If credentials stored in cloud ... [line truncated]\\n2472: - The `LegacyServiceAccountTokenNoAutoGeneration` feature gate is beta, and enabled by default. When enabled, Secret API objects containing service account tokens are no longer auto-generated for every ServiceAccount. Use the [TokenRequest](https://kuberne ... [line truncated]\\n2473: - The calculations for Pod topology spread skew now exclude nodes that\\n2474: ... [chunk truncated]\",\"endLine\":2482,\"startLine\":2470},{\"content\":\"3821: \\n3822:  - Docker runtime support using dockshim in the kubelet is now completely removed in 1.24. The kubelet used to have a a module called \\\"dockershim\\\" which implements CRI support for Docker and it has seen maintenance issues in the Kubernetes community. From  ... [line truncated]\\n3823:   - The calculations for Pod topology spread skew now excludes nodes that\\n3824:   don't match the node affinity/selector. This may lead to unschedulable pods if you previously had pods\\n3825:   matching the spreading selector on those excluded nodes (not matching the node affinity/selector),\\n3826:   especially when the topologyKey is not node-level. Revisit the node affinity and/or pod selector in the\\n3827:   topology spread constraints to avoid this scenario. ([#107009](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/107009), [@kerthcet](https://github.com/kerthcet)) [SIG Scheduling]\\n3828: \",\"endLine\":3828,\"startLine\":3821},{\"content\":\"2203: \\n2204: - EndpointSlices marked for deletion are now ignored during reconciliation. ([#110479](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernet ... [line truncated]\\n2206: - Pods will now post their readiness during termination. ([#110416](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110416), [@aojea](https://github.com/aojea)) [SIG Network, Node and Testing]\\n2207: - The pod phase lifecycle guarantees that terminal Pods, those whose states are Unready or Succeeded, can not regress and will have all container stopped. Hence, terminal Pods will never be reachable and should not publish their IP addresses on the Endpoin ... [line truncated]\\n2208: \\n2209: \",\"endLine\":2209,\"startLine\":2203}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.24.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.24.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"2608: - Client-go: make generated CA certificates valid 1 hour in the past (NewSelfSignedCACert). Applies to CA certificates and other certificates generated by kubeadm. ([#118631](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/118631), [@champtar](https://github ... [line truncated]\\n2609: - Fixes the alpha `CloudDualStackNodeIPs` feature. ([#118329](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/118329), [@danwinship](https://github.com/danwinship)) [SIG Network and Node]\\n2610: - Kubelet: un-deprecate --provider-id flag ([#116530](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/116530), [@pacoxu](https://github.com/pacoxu)) [SIG Node]\\n2611: - Migrated the Job controller (within `kube-controller-manager`) to use [contextual logging](https://k8s.io/docs/concepts/cluster-administration/system-logs/#contextual-logging). ([#116910](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/116910), [@fatsheep9 ... [line truncated]\\n2612: ... [chunk truncated]\",\"endLine\":2614,\"startLine\":2608},{\"content\":\"1449:   - apiserver_flowcontrol_current_executing_requests ([#119110](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/119110), [@andrewsykim](https://github.com/andrewsykim))\\n1450: - Renamed `PodHasNetwork` to `PodReadyToStartContainers`. ([#117702](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/117702), [@kannon92](https://github.com/kannon92)) [SIG Node and Testing]\\n1451: - Replaced `apiserver_storage_db_total_size_in_bytes` with `apiserver_storage_size_bytes` metric. ([#118812](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/118812), [@serathius](https://github.com/serathius))\\n1452: \",\"endLine\":1452,\"startLine\":1449},{\"content\":\"2562: \\n2563: ### Node   The possibility of borrowing means that the old metric `apiserver_flowcontrol_request_concurrency_limit` can no longer mean both the configured concurrency limit and the enforced concurrency limit. Henceforth it means the configured concurrency limit.' ( ... [line truncated]\\n2302: - '`NodeInclusionPolicy` in `podTopologySpread` plugin is now enabled by default.'\\n2303:    ([#113500](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/113500), [@kerthcet](https://github.com/kerthcet))\\n2304: - '`PodDisruptionBudget` now adds an alpha `spec.unhealthyPodEvictionPolicy` field.\\n2305:   When the `PDBUnhealthyPodEvictionPolicy` feature-gate is enabled in `kube-apiserver`,\\n2306:   setting this field to `\\\"AlwaysAllow\\\"` allows pods to be evicted if they do not\\n2307:   have a ready condition, regardless of whether the PodDisruptionBudget is currently\\n2308:   healthy.'\\n2309: \",\"endLine\":2309,\"startLine\":2301},{\"content\":\"2101: \\n2102: ### Node Binaries\\n2103: \\n2104: \",\"endLine\":2104,\"startLine\":2101},{\"content\":\"1535: \\n1536: ### Node Binaries\\n1537: \\n1538: \",\"endLine\":1538,\"startLine\":1535}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.26.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.26.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"36: \\teventsv1beta1 \\\"k8s.io/api/events/v1beta1\\\"\\n37: \\tnodev1beta1 \\\"k8s.io/api/node/v1beta1\\\"\\n38: \\tpolicyapiv1beta1 \\\"k8s.io/api/policy/v1beta1\\\"\\n39: \",\"endLine\":39,\"startLine\":36},{\"content\":\"84: \\t\\t\\tMasterCount:             1,\\n85: \\t\\t\\tEndpointReconcilerType:  reconcilers.MasterCountReconcilerType,\\n86: \\t\\t\\tServiceIPRange:          net.IPNet{IP: netutils.ParseIPSloppy(\\\"10.0.0.0\\\"), Mask: net.CIDRMask(24, 32)},\\n87: \",\"endLine\":87,\"startLine\":84},{\"content\":\"167: \\t\\t\\tClusterIPRange: apiserverCfg.ExtraConfig.ServiceIPRange,\\n168: \\t\\t\\tNodePortRange:  apiserverCfg.ExtraConfig.ServiceNodePortRange,\\n169: \\t\\t},\\n170: \",\"endLine\":170,\"startLine\":167}],\"file\":\"pkg/controlplane/instance_test.go\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/pkg/controlplane/instance_test.go\"}]}",

        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "612: \n613: // isNodeReady returns true if a node is ready; false otherwise.\n614: func isNodeReady(node *v1.Node) bool {\n615: \tfor _, c := range node.Status.Conditions {\n616: \t\tif c.Type == v1.NodeReady {\n617: \t\t\treturn c.Status == v1.ConditionTrue\n618: ",
            "endLine": 618,
            "startLine": 612
          },
          {
            "content": "532: \n533: func (c *Controller) updateNode(logger klog.Logger, old, cur interface{}) {\n534: \toldNode := old.(*v1.Node)\n535: \tcurNode := cur.(*v1.Node)\n536: \n537: \t// LabelTopologyZone may be added by cloud provider asynchronously after the Node is created.\n538: \t// The topology cache should be updated in this case.\n539: \tif isNodeReady(oldNode) != isNodeReady(curNode) ||\n540: \t\toldNode.Labels[v1.LabelTopologyZone] != curNode.Labels[v1.LabelTopologyZone] {\n541: \t\tc.checkNodeTopologyDistribution(logger)\n542: \t}\n543: ",
            "endLine": 543,
            "startLine": 532
          },
          {
            "content": "196: \n197: \t// podLister is able to list/get pods and is populated by the\n198: \t// shared informer passed to NewController\n199: \tpodLister corelisters.PodLister\n200: \t// podsSynced returns true if the pod shared informer has been synced at least once.\n201: \t// Added as a member to the struct to allow injection for testing.\n202: \tpodsSynced cache.InformerSynced\n203: \n204: \t// endpointSliceLister is able to list/get endpoint slices and is populated by the\n205: \t// shared informer passed to NewController\n206: \tendpointSliceLister discoverylisters.EndpointSliceLister\n207: \t// endpointSlicesSynced returns true if the endpoint slice shared informer has been synced at least once.\n208: \t// Added as a member to the struct to allow injection for testing.\n209: \tendpointSlicesSynced cache.InformerSynced\n210: \t// endpointSliceTracker tracks the list of EndpointSlices and associated\n211: \t// resource versions expected for each Service. It can help determine if a\n212: \t// cached EndpointSlice is out of date.\n213: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 225,
            "startLine": 196
          }
        ],
        "file": "pkg/controller/endpointslice/endpointslice_controller.go",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/pkg/controller/endpointslice/endpointslice_controller.go"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "78: \n79: // Reconcile takes a set of pods currently matching a service selector and\n80: // compares them with the endpoints already present in any existing endpoint\n81: // slices for the given service. It creates, updates, or deletes endpoint slices\n82: // to ensure the desired set of pods are represented by endpoint slices.\n83: func (r *Reconciler) Reconcile(logger klog.Logger, service *corev1.Service, pods []*corev1.Pod, existingSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, triggerTime time.Time) error {\n84: \tslicesToDelete := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}                                    // slices that are no longer  matching any address the service has\n85: \terrs := []error{}                                                                 // all errors generated in the process of reconciling\n86: \tslicesByAddressType := make(map[discovery.AddressType][]*discovery.EndpointSlice) // slices by address type\n87: \n88: ",
            "endLine": 88,
            "startLine": 78
          },
          {
            "content": "339: \n340: func NewReconciler(client clientset.Interface, nodeLister corelisters.NodeLister, maxEndpointsPerSlice int32, endpointSliceTracker *endpointsliceutil.EndpointSliceTracker, topologyCache *topologycache.TopologyCache, eventRecorder record.EventRecorder, cont ... [line truncated]\n341: \tr := &Reconciler{\n342: \t\tclient:               client,\n343: \t\tnodeLister:           nodeLister,\n344: \t\tmaxEndpointsPerSlice: maxEndpointsPerSlice,\n345: \t\tendpointSliceTracker: endpointSliceTracker,\n346: \t\tmetricsCache:         metrics.NewCache(maxEndpointsPerSlice),\n347: ",
            "endLine": 347,
            "startLine": 339
          },
          {
            "content": "194: \t\t\t}\n195: \t\t\t// If the Node specified by the Pod doesn't exist we want to requeue the Service so we\n196: \t\t\t// retry later, but also update the EndpointSlice without the problematic Pod.\n197: \t\t\t// Theoretically, the pod Garbage Collector will remove the Pod, but we want to avoid\n198: \t\t\t// situations where a reference from a Pod to a missing node can leave the EndpointSlice\n199: \t\t\t// stuck forever.\n200: \t\t\t// On the other side, if the service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses is set we just add the\n201: \t\t\t// Pod, since the user is explicitly indicating that the Pod address should be published.\n202: \t\t\tif !service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses {\n203: \t\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"skipping Pod for Service, Node not found\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"service\", klog.KObj(service), \"node\", klog.KRef(\"\", pod.Spec.NodeName))\n204: \t\t\t\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\"skipping Pod %s for Service %s/%s: Node %s Not Found\", pod.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, pod.Spec.NodeName))\n205: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n206: ",
            "endLine": 206,
            "startLine": 194
          }
        ],
        "file": "staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go"

          {
            "content": "397: \n398: // NodeLifecycleControllerConfiguration contains elements describing NodeLifecycleController.\n399: type NodeLifecycleControllerConfiguration struct {\n400: \t// nodeEvictionRate is the number of nodes per second on which pods are deleted in case of node failure when a zone is healthy\n401: \tNodeEvictionRate float32\n402: \t// secondaryNodeEvictionRate is the number of nodes per second on which pods are deleted in case of node failure when a zone is unhealthy\n403: \tSecondaryNodeEvictionRate float32\n404: \t// nodeStartupGracePeriod is the amount of time which we allow starting a node to\n405: \t// be unresponsive before marking it unhealthy.\n406: \tNodeStartupGracePeriod metav1.Duration\n407: \t// nodeMontiorGracePeriod is the amount of time which we allow a running node to be\n408: \t// unresponsive before marking it unhealthy. Must be N times more than kubelet's\n409: \t// nodeStatusUpdateFrequency, where N means number of retries allowed for kubelet\n410: \t// to post node status.\n411: \tNodeMonitorGracePeriod metav1.Duration\n412: ...           {

This is for development and testing only and\n42: \t// will not work in a multi-node cluster.\n43: \tPodTemplateFilePathHostPath string\n44: \t// minimumTimeoutHostPath is the minimum ActiveDeadlineSeconds to use for a HostPath\n45: \t// Recycler pod.             "content": "324: type HPAControllerConfiguration struct {\n325: \t// ConcurrentHorizontalPodAutoscalerSyncs is the number of HPA objects that are allowed to sync concurrently.\n326: \t// Larger number = more responsive HPA processing, but more CPU (and network) load.\n327: \tConcurrentHorizontalPodAutoscalerSyncs int32\n328: \t// HorizontalPodAutoscalerSyncPeriod is the period for syncing the number of\n329: \t// pods in horizontal pod autoscaler.\n330: \tHorizontalPodAutoscalerSyncPeriod metav1.Duration\n331: \t// HorizontalPodAutoscalerUpscaleForbiddenWindow is a period after which next upscale allowed.\n332: \tHorizontalPodAutoscalerUpscaleForbiddenWindow metav1.Duration\n333: \t// HorizontalPodAutoscalerDowncaleStabilizationWindow is a period for which autoscaler will look\n334: \t// backwards and not scale down below any recommendation it made during that period.\n335: \tHorizontalPodAutoscalerDownscaleStabilizationWindow metav1.Duration\n336: \t// HorizontalPodAutoscalerDownscaleForbiddenWindow is a period after which next downscale allowed.\n337: ...             "content": "1520: \n1521: // When a Pod references a Node that is not present in the informer cache the\n1522: // EndpointSlices will continue to allow create, updates and deletes through.\n1523: // The Pod with the missing reference will be published or retried depending of\n1524: // the service.spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses.\n1525: // The test considers two Pods on different nodes, one of the Nodes is not present.\n1526: func TestReconcilerPodMissingNode(t *testing.T) {\n1527: \tnamespace := \"test\"\n1528: ",

            "startLine": 1520
          },
          {

            "endLine": 696,
            "startLine": 691
          },
          {

      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "2208: \n2209: ### Workloads that saturate nodes with pods may see pods that fail due to node admission\n2210: \n2211: 1.22 addressed a long-standing issue in the Kubelet where terminating pods were [vulnerable to race conditions](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/102344) leading to early shutdown, resource leaks, or long delays in actually completing pod shutd ... [line truncated]\n2212: \n2213: ",
            "endLine": 2213,
            "startLine": 2208
          },
          {
            "content": "283:   - [Known Issues](#known-issues)\n284:     - [CPU and Memory manager are not working correctly for Guaranteed Pods with multiple containers](#cpu-and-memory-manager-are-not-working-correctly-for-guaranteed-pods-with-multiple-containers)\n285:     - [<code>CSIMigrationvSphere</code> feature gate has not migrated to new CRD APIs](#csimigrationvsphere-feature-gate-has-not-migrated-to-new-crd-apis)\n286:     - [Workloads that saturate nodes with pods may see pods that fail due to node admission](#workloads-that-saturate-nodes-with-pods-may-see-pods-that-fail-due-to-node-admission)\n287:     - [Etcd v3.5.[0-2] data corruption](#etcd-v350-2-data-corruption)\n288: ",
            "endLine": 288,
            "startLine": 283
          },
          {
            "content": "1158: \n1159: - Bug Fix: Kube-proxy dropped endpointSlice's local endpoints when upgrading from 1.20 to 1.22 ([#110245](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110245), [@xh4n3](https://github.com/xh4n3)) [SIG Network]\n1160: - EndpointSlices marked for deletion are now ignored during reconciliation. ([#110482](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110482), [@aryan9600](https://github.com/aryan9600)) [SIG Apps and Network]\n1161: - Fixed a kubelet issue that could result in invalid pod status updates to be sent to the api-server where pods would be reported in a terminal phase but also report a ready condition of true in some cases. ([#110481](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernet ... [line truncated]\n1162: - Pods will now post their readiness during termination. ([#110418](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110418), [@aojea](https://github.com/aojea)) [SIG Network, Node and Testing]\n1163: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 1165,
            "startLine": 1158
          }
        ],
        "file": "CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.22.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.22.md"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "3364: \n3365: - EndpointSlices with Pod referencing Nodes that doesn't exist couldn't be created or updated.\n3366:   The behavior on the EndpointSlice controller has been modified to update the EndpointSlice without the Pods that reference non-existing Nodes, and keep retrying until all Pods reference existing Nodes.\n3367:   However, if service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses is set, all the Pods are published without retrying.\n3368:   Fixed EndpointSlices metrics to reflect correctly the number of desired EndpointSlices when no endpoints are present. ([#110639](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110639), [@aojea](https://github.com/aojea)) [SIG Apps and Network]\n3369: \n3370: ",
            "endLine": 3370,
            "startLine": 3364
          },
          {
            "content": "2466: \n2467: - EndpointSlices with Pod referencing Nodes that don't exist couldn't be created or updated. The behavior on the EndpointSlice controller has been modified to update the EndpointSlice without the Pods that reference non-existing Nodes and keep retrying unt ... [line truncated]\n2468: - Optimization of kubectl Chinese translation ([#110538](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110538), [@hwdef](https://github.com/hwdef)) [SIG CLI]\n2469: ",
            "endLine": 2469,
            "startLine": 2466
          },
          {
            "content": "3584: - The kube-proxy `sync_proxy_rules_no_endpoints_total` metric now only counts local-traffic-policy services which have remote endpoints but not local endpoints. ([#109782](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/109782), [@danwinship](https://github. ... [line truncated]\n3585: - The pod phase lifecycle guarantees that terminal Pods, those whose states are Unready or Succeeded, can not regress and will have all container stopped. Hence, terminal Pods will never be reachable and should not publish their IP addresses on the Endpoin ... [line truncated]\n3586: - Upgrade Azure/go-autorest/autorest to v0.11.27 ([#110371](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110371), [@andyzhangx](https://github.com/andyzhangx)) [SIG Cloud Provider]\n3587: ",
            "endLine": 3587,
            "startLine": 3584
          }
        ],
        "file": "CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "2969: \n2970: - Add gRPC probe to Pod.Spec.Container.{Liveness,Readiness,Startup}Probe (#106463, @SergeyKanzhelev) [SIG API Machinery, Apps, CLI, Node and Testing]\n2971: - Adds a feature gate StatefulSetAutoDeletePVC, which allows PVCs automatically created for StatefulSet pods to be automatically deleted. (#99728, @mattcary) [SIG API Machinery, Apps, Auth and Testing]\n2972: - Performs strict server side schema validation requests via the `fieldValidation=[Strict,Warn,Ignore]` query parameter. (#105916, @kevindelgado) [SIG API Machinery, Apps, Auth, Cloud Provider and Testing]\n2973: - Support pod priority based node graceful shutdown (#102915, @wzshiming) [SIG Node and Testing]\n2974: \n2975: ",
            "endLine": 2975,
            "startLine": 2969
          },
          {
            "content": "1493: \n1494: - EndpointSlices marked for deletion are now ignored during reconciliation. ([#110483](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110483), [@aryan9600](https://github.com/aryan9600)) [SIG Apps and Network]\n1495: - Fixed a kubelet issue that could result in invalid pod status updates to be sent to the api-server where pods would be reported in a terminal phase but also report a ready condition of true in some cases. ([#110480](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernet ... [line truncated]\n1496: - Pods will now post their readiness during termination. ([#110417](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110417), [@aojea](https://github.com/aojea)) [SIG Network, Node and Testing]\n1497: - The pod phase lifecycle guarantees that terminal Pods, those whose states are Unready or Succeeded, can not regress and will have all container stopped. Hence, terminal Pods will never be reachable and should not publish their IP addresses on the Endpoin ... [line truncated]\n1498: \n1499: ",
            "endLine": 1499,
            "startLine": 1493
          },
          {
            "content": "3073: - KubeSchedulerConfiguration provides a new field `MultiPoint` which will register a plugin for all valid extension points (#105611, @damemi) [SIG Scheduling and Testing]\n3074: - Kubelet should reject pods whose OS doesn't match the node's OS label. (#105292, @ravisantoshgudimetla) [SIG Apps and Node]\n3075: - The CSIVolumeFSGroupPolicy feature has moved from beta to GA. (#105940, @dobsonj) [SIG Storage]\n3076: - The Kubelet's `--register-with-taints` option is now available via the Kubelet config file field registerWithTaints (#105437, @cmssczy) [SIG Node and Scalability]\n3077: - Validation rules for Custom Resource Definitions can be written in the [CEL expression language](https://github.com/google/cel-spec) using the `x-kubernetes-validations` extension in OpenAPIv3 schemas (alpha). This is gated by the alpha \"CustomResourceVa ... [line truncated]\n3078: \n3079: ",
            "endLine": 3079,
            "startLine": 3073
          }
        ],
        "file": "CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.23.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.23.md"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "1586: - kubeadm now deletes the bootstrap-kubelet.conf file after TLS bootstrap\n1587:   User relying on bootstrap-kubelet.conf should switch to kubelet.conf that contains node credentials ([#80676](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/80676), [@fabriziopandini](https://github.com/fabriziopandini))\n1588: - Node labels `beta.kubernetes.io/metadata-proxy-ready`, `beta.kubernetes.io/metadata-proxy-ready` and `beta.kubernetes.io/kube-proxy-ds-ready` are no longer added on new nodes.\n1589:   - ip-mask-agent addon starts to use the label `node.kubernetes.io/masq-agent-ds-ready` instead of `beta.kubernetes.io/masq-agent-ds-ready` as its node selector.\n1590:   - kube-proxy addon starts to use the label `node.kubernetes.io/kube-proxy-ds-ready` instead of `beta.kubernetes.io/kube-proxy-ds-ready` as its node selector.\n1591:   - metadata-proxy addon starts to use the label `cloud.google.com/metadata-proxy-ready` instead of `beta.kubernetes.io/metadata-proxy-ready` as its node selector.\n1592: \n1593: ",
            "endLine": 1593,
            "startLine": 1586
          },
          {
            "content": "2891: * Migrate scheduler to use v1beta1 Event API. action required: any tool targeting scheduler events needs to use v1beta1 Event API ([#78447](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/78447), [@yastij](https://github.com/yastij))\n2892: * scheduler.alpha.kubernetes.io/critical-pod annotation is removed. Pod priority (spec.priorityClassName) should be used instead to mark pods as critical. Action required! ([#79554](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/79554), [@draveness](https:/ ... [line truncated]\n2893: * hyperkube: the `--make-symlinks` flag, deprecated in v1.14, has been removed. ([#80017](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/80017), [@Pothulapati](https://github.com/Pothulapati))\n2894: * Node labels `beta.kubernetes.io/metadata-proxy-ready`, `beta.kubernetes.io/metadata-proxy-ready` and `beta.kubernetes.io/kube-proxy-ds-ready` are no longer added on new nodes. ([#79305](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/79305), [@paivagustavo ... [line truncated]\n2895: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 2900,
            "startLine": 2891
          },
          {
            "content": "1936: \n1937: ### Node\n1938: \n1939: ",
            "endLine": 1939,
            "startLine": 1936
          }
        ],
        "file": "CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.16.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.16.md"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "2470: - Docker runtime support using dockershim in the kubelet is now completely removed in 1.24. The kubelet used to have a module called dockershim, which implements CRI support for Docker, and it has seen maintenance issues in the Kubernetes community. From 1 ... [line truncated]\n2471: - Fixed bug with leads to Node goes `Not-ready` state when credentials for vCenter stored in a secret and Zones feature is in use. Zone labels setup moved to KCM component, kubelet skips this step during startup in such case. If credentials stored in cloud ... [line truncated]\n2472: - The `LegacyServiceAccountTokenNoAutoGeneration` feature gate is beta, and enabled by default. When enabled, Secret API objects containing service account tokens are no longer auto-generated for every ServiceAccount. Use the [TokenRequest](https://kuberne ... [line truncated]\n2473: - The calculations for Pod topology spread skew now exclude nodes that\n2474: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 2482,
            "startLine": 2470
          },
          {
            "content": "3821: \n3822:  - Docker runtime support using dockshim in the kubelet is now completely removed in 1.24. The kubelet used to have a a module called \"dockershim\" which implements CRI support for Docker and it has seen maintenance issues in the Kubernetes community. From  ... [line truncated]\n3823:   - The calculations for Pod topology spread skew now excludes nodes that\n3824:   don't match the node affinity/selector. This may lead to unschedulable pods if you previously had pods\n3825:   matching the spreading selector on those excluded nodes (not matching the node affinity/selector),\n3826:   especially when the topologyKey is not node-level. Revisit the node affinity and/or pod selector in the\n3827:   topology spread constraints to avoid this scenario. ([#107009](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/107009), [@kerthcet](https://github.com/kerthcet)) [SIG Scheduling]\n3828: ",
            "endLine": 3828,
            "startLine": 3821
          },
          {
            "content": "2203: \n2204: - EndpointSlices marked for deletion are now ignored during reconciliation. ([#110484](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110484), [@aryan9600](https://github.com/aryan9600)) [SIG Apps and Network]\n2205: - Fixed a kubelet issue that could result in invalid pod status updates to be sent to the api-server where pods would be reported in a terminal phase but also report a ready condition of true in some cases. ([#110479](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernet ... [line truncated]\n2206: - Pods will now post their readiness during termination. ([#110416](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110416), [@aojea](https://github.com/aojea)) [SIG Network, Node and Testing]\n2207: - The pod phase lifecycle guarantees that terminal Pods, those whose states are Unready or Succeeded, can not regress and will have all container stopped. Hence, terminal Pods will never be reachable and should not publish their IP addresses on the Endpoin ... [line truncated]\n2208: \n2209: ",
            "endLine": 2209,
            "startLine": 2203

        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "2608: - Client-go: make generated CA certificates valid 1 hour in the past (NewSelfSignedCACert). Applies to CA certificates and other certificates generated by kubeadm. ([#118631](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/118631), [@champtar](https://github ... [line truncated]\n2609: - Fixes the alpha `CloudDualStackNodeIPs` feature. ([#118329](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/118329), [@danwinship](https://github.com/danwinship)) [SIG Network and Node]\n2610: - Kubelet: un-deprecate --provider-id flag ([#116530](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/116530), [@pacoxu](https://github.com/pacoxu)) [SIG Node]\n2611: - Migrated the Job controller (within `kube-controller-manager`) to use [contextual logging](https://k8s.io/docs/concepts/cluster-administration/system-logs/#contextual-logging). ([#116910](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/116910), [@fatsheep9 ... [line truncated]\n2612: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 2614,
            "startLine": 2608

            "content": "2301:   The possibility of borrowing means that the old metric `apiserver_flowcontrol_request_concurrency_limit` can no longer mean both the configured concurrency limit and the enforced concurrency limit. Henceforth it means the configured concurrency limit.' ( ... [line truncated]\n2302: - '`NodeInclusionPolicy` in `podTopologySpread` plugin is now enabled by default.'\n2303:    ([#113500](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/113500), [@kerthcet](https://github.com/kerthcet))\n2304: - '`PodDisruptionBudget` now adds an alpha `spec.unhealthyPodEvictionPolicy` field.\n2305:   When the `PDBUnhealthyPodEvictionPolicy` feature-gate is enabled in `kube-apiserver`,\n2306:   setting this field to `\"AlwaysAllow\"` allows pods to be evicted if they do not\n2307:   have a ready condition, regardless of whether the PodDisruptionBudget is currently\n2308:   healthy.'\n2309: ",

endpoints already present in any existing endpoint\\n81: // slices for the given service. It creates, updates, or deletes endpoint slices\\n82: // to ensure the desired set of pods are represented by endpoint slices.\\n83: func (r *Reconciler) Reconcile(logger klog.Logger, service *corev1.Service, pods []*corev1.Pod, existingSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, triggerTime time.Time) error {\\n84: \\tslicesToDelete := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}                                    // slices that are no longer  matching any address the service has\\n85: \\terrs := []error{}                                                                 // all errors generated in the process of reconciling\\n86: \\tslicesByAddressType := make(map[discovery.AddressType][]*discovery.EndpointSlice) // slices by address type\\n87: \\n88: \\t// addresses that this service supports [o(1) find]\\n89: \\tserviceSupportedAddressesTypes := getAddressTypesForService(logger, service)\\n90: \\n91: \\t// loop through slices identifying their address type.\\n92: \\t// slices that no longer match address type supported by services\\n93: \\t// go to delete, other slices goes to the Reconciler machinery\\n94: \\t// for further adjustment\\n95: \\tfor _, existingSlice := range existingSlices {\\n96: \\t\\t// service no longer supports that address type, add it to deleted slices\\n97: \\t\\tif !serviceSupportedAddressesTypes.Has(existingSlice.AddressType) {\\n98: \\t\\t\\tif r.topologyCache != nil {\\n99: \\t\\t\\t\\tsvcKey, err := ServiceControllerKey(existingSlice)\\n100: \\t\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go%3FL1-100\"}",

    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2019 The Kubernetes Authors.\n3: \n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14: limitations under the License.\n15: */\n16: \n17: package endpointslice\n18: \n19: import (\n20: \t\"context\"\n21: \t\"fmt\"\n22: \t\"sort\"\n23: \t\"time\"\n24: \n25: \tcorev1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n26: \tdiscovery \"k8s.io/api/discovery/v1\"\n27: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n28: \tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n29: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/conversion\"\n30: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n31: \tutilerrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/errors\"\n32: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n33: \tclientset \"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes\"\n34: \tcorelisters \"k8s.io/client-go/listers/core/v1\"\n35: \t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/record\"\n36: \t\"k8s.io/endpointslice/metrics\"\n37: \t\"k8s.io/endpointslice/topologycache\"\n38: \t\"k8s.io/endpointslice/trafficdist\"\n39: \tendpointsliceutil \"k8s.io/endpointslice/util\"\n40: \t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n41: )\n42: \n43: // Reconciler is responsible for transforming current EndpointSlice state into\n44: // desired state\n45: type Reconciler struct {\n46: \tclient               clientset.Interface\n47: \tnodeLister           corelisters.NodeLister\n48: \tmaxEndpointsPerSlice int32\n49: \tendpointSliceTracker *endpointsliceutil.EndpointSliceTracker\n50: \tmetricsCache         *metrics.Cache\n51: \t// topologyCache tracks the distribution of Nodes and endpoints across zones\n52: \t// to enable TopologyAwareHints.\n53: \ttopologyCache *topologycache.TopologyCache\n54: \t// trafficDistributionEnabled determines if endpointDistribution field is to\n55: \t// be considered when reconciling EndpointSlice hints.\n56: \ttrafficDistributionEnabled bool\n57: \t// eventRecorder allows Reconciler to record and publish events.\n58: \teventRecorder  record.EventRecorder\n59: \tcontrollerName string\n60: }\n61: \n62: type ReconcilerOption func(*Reconciler)\n63: \n64: // WithTrafficDistributionEnabled controls whether the Reconciler considers the\n65: // `trafficDistribution` field while reconciling EndpointSlices.\n66: func WithTrafficDistributionEnabled(enabled bool) ReconcilerOption {\n67: \treturn func(r *Reconciler) {\n68: \t\tr.trafficDistributionEnabled = enabled\n69: \t}\n70: }\n71: \n72: // endpointMeta includes the attributes we group slices on, this type helps with\n73: // that logic in Reconciler\n74: type endpointMeta struct {\n75: \tports       []discovery.EndpointPort\n76: \taddressType discovery.AddressType\n77: }\n78: \n79: // Reconcile takes a set of pods currently matching a service selector and\n80: // compares them with the endpoints already present in any existing endpoint\n81: // slices for the given service. It creates, updates, or deletes endpoint slices\n82: // to ensure the desired set of pods are represented by endpoint slices.\n83: func (r *Reconciler) Reconcile(logger klog.Logger, service *corev1.Service, pods []*corev1.Pod, existingSlices []*discovery.EndpointSlice, triggerTime time.Time) error {\n84: \tslicesToDelete := []*discovery.EndpointSlice{}                                    // slices that are no longer  matching any address the service has\n85: \terrs := []error{}                                                                 // all errors generated in the process of reconciling\n86: \tslicesByAddressType := make(map[discovery.AddressType][]*discovery.EndpointSlice) // slices by address type\n87: \n88: \t// addresses that this service supports [o(1) find]\n89: \tserviceSupportedAddressesTypes := getAddressTypesForService(logger, service)\n90: \n91: \t// loop through slices identifying their address type.\n92: \t// slices that no longer match address type supported by services\n93: \t// go to delete, other slices goes to the Reconciler machinery\n94: \t// for further adjustment\n95: \tfor _, existingSlice := range existingSlices {\n96: \t\t// service no longer supports that address type, add it to deleted slices\n97: \t\tif !serviceSupportedAddressesTypes.Has(existingSlice.AddressType) {\n98: \t\t\tif r.topologyCache != nil {\n99: \t\t\t\tsvcKey, err := ServiceControllerKey(existingSlice)\n100: \t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n101: ",

            "content": "35: \n36: // podToEndpoint returns an Endpoint object generated from a Pod, a Node, and a Service for a particular addressType.\n37: func podToEndpoint(pod *v1.Pod, node *v1.Node, service *v1.Service, addressType discovery.AddressType) discovery.Endpoint {\n38: \tserving := endpointutil.IsPodReady(pod)\n39: \tterminating := pod.DeletionTimestamp != nil\n40: \t// For compatibility reasons, \"ready\" should never be \"true\" if a pod is terminatng, unless\n41: \t// publishNotReadyAddresses was set.\n42: \tready := service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses || (serving && !terminating)\n43: \tep := discovery.Endpoint{\n44: ",
