  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2: Copyright 2019 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n3: \\n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n7: \\n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n9: \\n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n14: limitations under the License.\\n15: */\\n16: \\n17: package util\\n18: \\n19: import (\\n20: \\t\\\"crypto/md5\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"encoding/hex\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n23: \\t\\\"hash\\\"\\n24: \\t\\\"reflect\\\"\\n25: \\t\\\"sort\\\"\\n26: \\n27: \\t\\\"github.com/davecgh/go-spew/spew\\\"\\n28: \\tv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n29: \\tdiscovery \\\"k8s.io/api/discovery/v1\\\"\\n30: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/conversion\\\"\\n31: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\\\"\\n32: \\tutilruntime \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\\\"\\n33: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n34: \\tv1listers \\\"k8s.io/client-go/listers/core/v1\\\"\\n35: \\t\\\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache\\\"\\n36: )\\n37: \\n38: // semanticIgnoreResourceVersion does semantic deep equality checks for objects\\n39: // but excludes ResourceVersion of ObjectReference. They are used when comparing\\n40: // endpoints in Endpoints and EndpointSlice objects to avoid unnecessary updates\\n41: // caused by Pod resourceVersion change.\\n42: var semanticIgnoreResourceVersion = conversion.EqualitiesOrDie(\\n43: \\tfunc(a, b v1.ObjectReference) bool {\\n44: \\t\\ta.ResourceVersion = \\\"\\\"\\n45: \\t\\tb.ResourceVersion = \\\"\\\"\\n46: \\t\\treturn a == b\\n47: \\t},\\n48: )\\n49: \\n50: // GetPodServiceMemberships returns a set of Service keys for Services that have\\n51: // a selector matching the given pod.\\n52: func GetPodServiceMemberships(serviceLister v1listers.ServiceLister, pod *v1.Pod) (sets.String, error) {\\n53: \\tset := sets.String{}\\n54: \\tservices, err := serviceLister.Services(pod.Namespace).List(labels.Everything())\\n55: \\tif err != nil {\\n56: \\t\\treturn set, err\\n57: \\t}\\n58: \\n59: \\tfor _, service := range services {\\n60: \\t\\tif service.Spec.Selector == nil {\\n61: \\t\\t\\t// if the service has a nil selector this means selectors match nothing, not everything.\\n62: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n63: \\t\\t}\\n64: \\t\\tkey, err := cache.DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc(service)\\n65: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n66: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n67: \\t\\t}\\n68: \\t\\tif labels.ValidatedSetSelector(service.Spec.Selector).Matches(labels.Set(pod.Labels)) {\\n69: \\t\\t\\tset.Insert(key)\\n70: \\t\\t}\\n71: \\t}\\n72: \\treturn set, nil\\n73: }\\n74: \\n75: // PortMapKey is used to uniquely identify groups of endpoint ports.\\n76: type PortMapKey string\\n77: \\n78: // NewPortMapKey generates a PortMapKey from endpoint ports.\\n79: func NewPortMapKey(endpointPorts []discovery.EndpointPort) PortMapKey {\\n80: \\tsort.Sort(portsInOrder(endpointPorts))\\n81: \\treturn PortMapKey(deepHashObjectToString(endpointPorts))\\n82: }\\n83: \\n84: // deepHashObjectToString creates a unique hash string from a go object.\\n85: func deepHashObjectToString(objectToWrite interface{}) string {\\n86: \\thasher := md5.New()\\n87: \\tdeepHashObject(hasher, objectToWrite)\\n88: \\treturn hex.EncodeToString(hasher.Sum(nil)[0:])\\n89: }\\n90: \\n91: // ShouldPodBeInEndpoints returns true if a specified pod should be in an\\n92: // Endpoints or EndpointSlice resource. Terminating pods are only included if\\n93: // includeTerminating is true.\\n94: func ShouldPodBeInEndpoints(pod *v1.Pod, includeTerminating bool) bool {\\n95: \\t// \\\"Terminal\\\" describes when a Pod is complete (in a succeeded or failed phase).\\n96: \\t// This is distinct from the \\\"Terminating\\\" condition which represents when a Pod\\n97: \\t// is being terminated (metadata.deletionTimestamp is non nil).\\n98: \\tif isPodTerminal(pod) {\\n99: \\t\\treturn false\\n100: \\t}\\n101: \\n102: \\tif len(pod.Status.PodIP) == 0 && len(pod.Status.PodIPs) == 0 {\\n103: \\t\\treturn false\\n104: \\t}\\n105: \\n106: \\tif !includeTerminating && pod.DeletionTimestamp != nil {\\n107: \\t\\treturn false\\n108: \\t}\\n109: \\n110: \\treturn true\\n111: }\\n112: \\n113: // ShouldSetHostname returns true if the Hostname attribute should be set on an\\n114: // Endpoints Address or EndpointSlice Endpoint.\\n115: func ShouldSetHostname(pod *v1.Pod, svc *v1.Service) bool {\\n116: \\treturn len(pod.Spec.Hostname) > 0 && pod.Spec.Subdomain == svc.Name && svc.Namespace == pod.Namespace\\n117: }\\n118: \\n119: // podEndpointsChanged returns two boolean values. The first is true if the pod has\\n120: // changed in a way that may change existing endpoints. The second value is true if the\\n121: // pod has changed in a way that may affect which Services it matches.\\n122: func podEndpointsChanged(oldPod, newPod *v1.Pod) (bool, bool) {\\n123: \\t// Check if the pod labels have changed, indicating a possible\\n124: \\t// change in the service membership\\n125: \\tlabelsChanged := false\\n126: \\tif !reflect.DeepEqual(newPod.Labels, oldPod.Labels) ||\\n127: \\t\\t!hostNameAndDomainAreEqual(newPod, oldPod) {\\n128: \\t\\tlabelsChanged = true\\n129: \\t}\\n130: \\n131: \\t// If the pod's deletion timestamp is set, remove endpoint from ready address.\\n132: \\tif newPod.DeletionTimestamp != oldPod.DeletionTimestamp {\\n133: \\t\\treturn true, labelsChanged\\n134: \\t}\\n135: \\t// If the pod's readiness has changed, the associated endpoint address\\n136: \\t// will move from the unready endpoints set to the ready endpoints.\\n137: \\t// So for the purposes of an endpoint, a readiness change on a pod\\n138: \\t// means we have a changed pod.\\n139: \\tif IsPodReady(oldPod) != IsPodReady(newPod) {\\n140: \\t\\treturn true, labelsChanged\\n141: \\t}\\n142: \\n143: \\t// Check if the pod IPs have changed\\n144: \\tif len(oldPod.Status.PodIPs) != len(newPod.Status.PodIPs) {\\n145: \\t\\treturn true, labelsChanged\\n146: \\t}\\n147: \\tfor i := range oldPod.Status.PodIPs {\\n148: \\t\\tif oldPod.Status.PodIPs[i].IP != newPod.Status.PodIPs[i].IP {\\n149: \\t\\t\\treturn true, labelsChanged\\n150: \\t\\t}\\n151: \\t}\\n152: \\n153: \\t// Endpoints may also reference a pod's Name, Namespace, UID, and NodeName, but\\n154: \\t// the first three are immutable, and NodeName is immutable once initially set,\\n155: \\t// which happens before the pod gets an IP.\\n156: \\n157: \\treturn false, labelsChanged\\n158: }\\n159: \\n160: // GetServicesToUpdateOnPodChange returns a set of Service keys for Services\\n161: // that have potentially been affected by a change to this pod.\\n162: func GetServicesToUpdateOnPodChange(serviceLister v1listers.ServiceLister, old, cur interface{}) sets.String {\\n163: \\tnewPod := cur.(*v1.Pod)\\n164: \\toldPod := old.(*v1.Pod)\\n165: \\tif newPod.ResourceVersion == oldPod.ResourceVersion {\\n166: \\t\\t// Periodic resync will send update events for all known pods.\\n167: \\t\\t// Two different versions of the same pod will always have different RVs\\n168: \\t\\treturn sets.String{}\\n169: \\t}\\n170: \\n171: \\tpodChanged, labelsChanged := podEndpointsChanged(oldPod, newPod)\\n172: \\n173: \\t// If both the pod and labels are unchanged, no update is needed\\n174: \\tif !podChanged && !labelsChanged {\\n175: \\t\\treturn sets.String{}\\n176: \\t}\\n177: \\n178: \\tservices, err := GetPodServiceMemberships(serviceLister, newPod)\\n179: \\tif err != nil {\\n180: \\t\\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"unable to get pod %s/%s's service memberships: %v\\\", newPod.Namespace, newPod.Name, err))\\n181: \\t\\treturn sets.String{}\\n182: \\t}\\n183: \\n184: \\tif labelsChanged {\\n185: \\t\\toldServices, err := GetPodServiceMemberships(serviceLister, oldPod)\\n186: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n187: \\t\\t\\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"unable to get pod %s/%s's service memberships: %v\\\", oldPod.Namespace, oldPod.Name, err))\\n188: \\t\\t}\\n189: \\t\\tservices = determineNeededServiceUpdates(oldServices, services, podChanged)\\n190: \\t}\\n191: \\n192: \\treturn services\\n193: }\\n194: \\n195: // GetPodFromDeleteAction returns a pointer to a pod if one can be derived from\\n196: // obj (could be a *v1.Pod, or a DeletionFinalStateUnknown marker item).\\n197: func GetPodFromDeleteAction(obj interface{}) *v1.Pod {\\n198: \\tif pod, ok := obj.(*v1.Pod); ok {\\n199: \\t\\t// Enqueue all the services that the pod used to be a member of.\\n200: \\t\\t// This is the same thing we do when we add a pod.\\n201: \\t\\treturn pod\\n202: \\t}\\n203: \\t// If we reached here it means the pod was deleted but its final state is unrecorded.\\n204: \\ttombstone, ok := obj.(cache.DeletedFinalStateUnknown)\\n205: \\tif !ok {\\n206: \\t\\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"couldn't get object from tombstone %#v\\\", obj))\\n207: \\t\\treturn nil\\n208: \\t}\\n209: \\tpod, ok := tombstone.Obj.(*v1.Pod)\\n210: \\tif !ok {\\n211: \\t\\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\\\"tombstone contained object that is not a Pod: %#v\\\", obj))\\n212: \\t\\treturn nil\\n213: \\t}\\n214: \\treturn pod\\n215: }\\n216: \\n217: func hostNameAndDomainAreEqual(pod1, pod2 *v1.Pod) bool {\\n218: \\treturn pod1.Spec.Hostname == pod2.Spec.Hostname &&\\n219: \\t\\tpod1.Spec.Subdomain == pod2.Spec.Subdomain\\n220: }\\n221: \\n222: func determineNeededServiceUpdates(oldServices, services sets.String, podChanged bool) sets.String {\\n223: \\tif podChanged {\\n224: \\t\\t// if the labels and pod changed, all services need to be updated\\n225: \\t\\tservices = services.Union(oldServices)\\n226: \\t} else {\\n227: \\t\\t// if only the labels changed, services not common to both the new\\n228: \\t\\t// and old service set (the disjuntive union) need to be updated\\n229: \\t\\tservices = services.Difference(oldServices).Union(oldServices.Difference(services))\\n230: \\t}\\n231: \\treturn services\\n232: }\\n233: \\n234: // portsInOrder helps sort endpoint ports in a consistent way for hashing.\\n235: type portsInOrder []discovery.EndpointPort\\n236: \\n237: func (sl portsInOrder) Len() int      { return len(sl) }\\n238: func (sl portsInOrder) Swap(i, j int) { sl[i], sl[j] = sl[j], sl[i] }\\n239: func (sl portsInOrder) Less(i, j int) bool {\\n240: \\th1 := deepHashObjectToString(sl[i])\\n241: \\th2 := deepHashObjectToString(sl[j])\\n242: \\treturn h1 < h2\\n243: }\\n244: \\n245: // EndpointsEqualBeyondHash returns true if endpoints have equal attributes\\n246: // but excludes equality checks that would have already been covered with\\n247: // endpoint hashing (see hashEndpoint func for more info) and ignores difference\\n248: // in ResourceVersion of TargetRef.\\n249: func EndpointsEqualBeyondHash(ep1, ep2 *discovery.Endpoint) bool {\\n250: \\tif stringPtrChanged(ep1.NodeName, ep2.NodeName) {\\n251: \\t\\treturn false\\n252: \\t}\\n253: \\n254: \\tif stringPtrChanged(ep1.Zone, ep2.Zone) {\\n255: \\t\\treturn false\\n256: \\t}\\n257: \\n258: \\tif boolPtrChanged(ep1.Conditions.Ready, ep2.Conditions.Ready) {\\n259: \\t\\treturn false\\n260: \\t}\\n261: \\n262: \\tif boolPtrChanged(ep1.Conditions.Serving, ep2.Conditions.Serving) {\\n263: \\t\\treturn false\\n264: \\t}\\n265: \\n266: \\tif boolPtrChanged(ep1.Conditions.Terminating, ep2.Conditions.Terminating) {\\n267: \\t\\treturn false\\n268: \\t}\\n269: \\n270: \\tif !semanticIgnoreResourceVersion.DeepEqual(ep1.TargetRef, ep2.TargetRef) {\\n271: \\t\\treturn false\\n272: \\t}\\n273: \\n274: \\treturn true\\n275: }\\n276: \\n277: // boolPtrChanged returns true if a set of bool pointers have different values.\\n278: func boolPtrChanged(ptr1, ptr2 *bool) bool {\\n279: \\tif (ptr1 == nil) != (ptr2 == nil) {\\n280: \\t\\treturn true\\n281: \\t}\\n282: \\tif ptr1 != nil && ptr2 != nil && *ptr1 != *ptr2 {\\n283: \\t\\treturn true\\n284: \\t}\\n285: \\treturn false\\n286: }\\n287: \\n288: // stringPtrChanged returns true if a set of string pointers have different values.\\n289: func stringPtrChanged(ptr1, ptr2 *string) bool {\\n290: \\tif (ptr1 == nil) != (ptr2 == nil) {\\n291: \\t\\treturn true\\n292: \\t}\\n293: \\tif ptr1 != nil && ptr2 != nil && *ptr1 != *ptr2 {\\n294: \\t\\treturn true\\n295: \\t}\\n296: \\treturn false\\n297: }\\n298: \\n299: // DeepHashObject writes specified object to hash using the spew library\\n300: // which follows pointers and prints actual values of the nested objects\\n301: // ensuring the hash does not change when a pointer changes.\\n302: // copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/util/hash\\n303: func deepHashObject(hasher hash.Hash, objectToWrite interface{}) {\\n304: \\thasher.Reset()\\n305: \\tprinter := spew.ConfigState{\\n306: \\t\\tIndent:         \\\" \\\",\\n307: \\t\\tSortKeys:       true,\\n308: \\t\\tDisableMethods: true,\\n309: \\t\\tSpewKeys:       true,\\n310: \\t}\\n311: \\tprinter.Fprintf(hasher, \\\"%#v\\\", objectToWrite)\\n312: }\\n313: \\n314: // IsPodReady returns true if Pods Ready condition is true\\n315: // copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\\n316: func IsPodReady(pod *v1.Pod) bool {\\n317: \\treturn isPodReadyConditionTrue(pod.Status)\\n318: }\\n319: \\n320: // IsPodTerminal returns true if a pod is terminal, all containers are stopped and cannot ever regress.\\n321: // copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\\n322: func isPodTerminal(pod *v1.Pod) bool {\\n323: \\treturn isPodPhaseTerminal(pod.Status.Phase)\\n324: }\\n325: \\n326: // IsPodPhaseTerminal returns true if the pod's phase is terminal.\\n327: // copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\\n328: func isPodPhaseTerminal(phase v1.PodPhase) bool {\\n329: \\treturn phase == v1.PodFailed || phase == v1.PodSucceeded\\n330: }\\n331: \\n332: // IsPodReadyConditionTrue returns true if a pod is ready; false otherwise.\\n333: // copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\\n334: func isPodReadyConditionTrue(status v1.PodStatus) bool {\\n335: \\tcondition := getPodReadyCondition(&status)\\n336: \\treturn condition != nil && condition.Status == v1.ConditionTrue\\n337: }\\n338: \\n339: // getPodReadyCondition extracts the pod ready condition from the given status and returns that.\\n340: // Returns nil if the condition is not present.\\n341: // copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\\n342: func getPodReadyCondition(status *v1.PodStatus) *v1.PodCondition {\\n343: \\tif status == nil || status.Conditions == nil {\\n344: \\t\\treturn nil\\n345: \\t}\\n346: \\n347: \\tfor i := range status.Conditions {\\n348: \\t\\tif status.Conditions[i].Type == v1.PodReady {\\n349: \\t\\t\\treturn &status.Conditions[i]\\n350: \\t\\t}\\n351: \\t}\\n352: \\treturn nil\\n353: }\\n354: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/util/controller_utils.go\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2019 The Kubernetes Authors.\n3: \n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\n7: \n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9: \n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14: limitations under the License.\n15: */\n16: \n17: package util\n18: \n19: import (\n20: \t\"crypto/md5\"\n21: \t\"encoding/hex\"\n22: \t\"fmt\"\n23: \t\"hash\"\n24: \t\"reflect\"\n25: \t\"sort\"\n26: \n27: \t\"github.com/davecgh/go-spew/spew\"\n28: \tv1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n29: \tdiscovery \"k8s.io/api/discovery/v1\"\n30: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/conversion\"\n31: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\"\n32: \tutilruntime \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\"\n33: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n34: \tv1listers \"k8s.io/client-go/listers/core/v1\"\n35: \t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache\"\n36: )\n37: \n38: // semanticIgnoreResourceVersion does semantic deep equality checks for objects\n39: // but excludes ResourceVersion of ObjectReference. They are used when comparing\n40: // endpoints in Endpoints and EndpointSlice objects to avoid unnecessary updates\n41: // caused by Pod resourceVersion change.\n42: var semanticIgnoreResourceVersion = conversion.EqualitiesOrDie(\n43: \tfunc(a, b v1.ObjectReference) bool {\n44: \t\ta.ResourceVersion = \"\"\n45: \t\tb.ResourceVersion = \"\"\n46: \t\treturn a == b\n47: \t},\n48: )\n49: \n50: // GetPodServiceMemberships returns a set of Service keys for Services that have\n51: // a selector matching the given pod.\n52: func GetPodServiceMemberships(serviceLister v1listers.ServiceLister, pod *v1.Pod) (sets.String, error) {\n53: \tset := sets.String{}\n54: \tservices, err := serviceLister.Services(pod.Namespace).List(labels.Everything())\n55: \tif err != nil {\n56: \t\treturn set, err\n57: \t}\n58: \n59: \tfor _, service := range services {\n60: \t\tif service.Spec.Selector == nil {\n61: \t\t\t// if the service has a nil selector this means selectors match nothing, not everything.\n62: \t\t\tcontinue\n63: \t\t}\n64: \t\tkey, err := cache.DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc(service)\n65: \t\tif err != nil {\n66: \t\t\treturn nil, err\n67: \t\t}\n68: \t\tif labels.ValidatedSetSelector(service.Spec.Selector).Matches(labels.Set(pod.Labels)) {\n69: \t\t\tset.Insert(key)\n70: \t\t}\n71: \t}\n72: \treturn set, nil\n73: }\n74: \n75: // PortMapKey is used to uniquely identify groups of endpoint ports.\n76: type PortMapKey string\n77: \n78: // NewPortMapKey generates a PortMapKey from endpoint ports.\n79: func NewPortMapKey(endpointPorts []discovery.EndpointPort) PortMapKey {\n80: \tsort.Sort(portsInOrder(endpointPorts))\n81: \treturn PortMapKey(deepHashObjectToString(endpointPorts))\n82: }\n83: \n84: // deepHashObjectToString creates a unique hash string from a go object.\n85: func deepHashObjectToString(objectToWrite interface{}) string {\n86: \thasher := md5.New()\n87: \tdeepHashObject(hasher, objectToWrite)\n88: \treturn hex.EncodeToString(hasher.Sum(nil)[0:])\n89: }\n90: \n91: // ShouldPodBeInEndpoints returns true if a specified pod should be in an\n92: // Endpoints or EndpointSlice resource. Terminating pods are only included if\n93: // includeTerminating is true.\n94: func ShouldPodBeInEndpoints(pod *v1.Pod, includeTerminating bool) bool {\n95: \t// \"Terminal\" describes when a Pod is complete (in a succeeded or failed phase).\n96: \t// This is distinct from the \"Terminating\" condition which represents when a Pod\n97: \t// is being terminated (metadata.deletionTimestamp is non nil).\n98: \tif isPodTerminal(pod) {\n99: \t\treturn false\n100: \t}\n101: \n102: \tif len(pod.Status.PodIP) == 0 && len(pod.Status.PodIPs) == 0 {\n103: \t\treturn false\n104: \t}\n105: \n106: \tif !includeTerminating && pod.DeletionTimestamp != nil {\n107: \t\treturn false\n108: \t}\n109: \n110: \treturn true\n111: }\n112: \n113: // ShouldSetHostname returns true if the Hostname attribute should be set on an\n114: // Endpoints Address or EndpointSlice Endpoint.\n115: func ShouldSetHostname(pod *v1.Pod, svc *v1.Service) bool {\n116: \treturn len(pod.Spec.Hostname) > 0 && pod.Spec.Subdomain == svc.Name && svc.Namespace == pod.Namespace\n117: }\n118: \n119: // podEndpointsChanged returns two boolean values. The first is true if the pod has\n120: // changed in a way that may change existing endpoints. The second value is true if the\n121: // pod has changed in a way that may affect which Services it matches.\n122: func podEndpointsChanged(oldPod, newPod *v1.Pod) (bool, bool) {\n123: \t// Check if the pod labels have changed, indicating a possible\n124: \t// change in the service membership\n125: \tlabelsChanged := false\n126: \tif !reflect.DeepEqual(newPod.Labels, oldPod.Labels) ||\n127: \t\t!hostNameAndDomainAreEqual(newPod, oldPod) {\n128: \t\tlabelsChanged = true\n129: \t}\n130: \n131: \t// If the pod's deletion timestamp is set, remove endpoint from ready address.\n132: \tif newPod.DeletionTimestamp != oldPod.DeletionTimestamp {\n133: \t\treturn true, labelsChanged\n134: \t}\n135: \t// If the pod's readiness has changed, the associated endpoint address\n136: \t// will move from the unready endpoints set to the ready endpoints.\n137: \t// So for the purposes of an endpoint, a readiness change on a pod\n138: \t// means we have a changed pod.\n139: \tif IsPodReady(oldPod) != IsPodReady(newPod) {\n140: \t\treturn true, labelsChanged\n141: \t}\n142: \n143: \t// Check if the pod IPs have changed\n144: \tif len(oldPod.Status.PodIPs) != len(newPod.Status.PodIPs) {\n145: \t\treturn true, labelsChanged\n146: \t}\n147: \tfor i := range oldPod.Status.PodIPs {\n148: \t\tif oldPod.Status.PodIPs[i].IP != newPod.Status.PodIPs[i].IP {\n149: \t\t\treturn true, labelsChanged\n150: \t\t}\n151: \t}\n152: \n153: \t// Endpoints may also reference a pod's Name, Namespace, UID, and NodeName, but\n154: \t// the first three are immutable, and NodeName is immutable once initially set,\n155: \t// which happens before the pod gets an IP.\n156: \n157: \treturn false, labelsChanged\n158: }\n159: \n160: // GetServicesToUpdateOnPodChange returns a set of Service keys for Services\n161: // that have potentially been affected by a change to this pod.\n162: func GetServicesToUpdateOnPodChange(serviceLister v1listers.ServiceLister, old, cur interface{}) sets.String {\n163: \tnewPod := cur.(*v1.Pod)\n164: \toldPod := old.(*v1.Pod)\n165: \tif newPod.ResourceVersion == oldPod.ResourceVersion {\n166: \t\t// Periodic resync will send update events for all known pods.\n167: \t\t// Two different versions of the same pod will always have different RVs\n168: \t\treturn sets.String{}\n169: \t}\n170: \n171: \tpodChanged, labelsChanged := podEndpointsChanged(oldPod, newPod)\n172: \n173: \t// If both the pod and labels are unchanged, no update is needed\n174: \tif !podChanged && !labelsChanged {\n175: \t\treturn sets.String{}\n176: \t}\n177: \n178: \tservices, err := GetPodServiceMemberships(serviceLister, newPod)\n179: \tif err != nil {\n180: \t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"unable to get pod %s/%s's service memberships: %v\", newPod.Namespace, newPod.Name, err))\n181: \t\treturn sets.String{}\n182: \t}\n183: \n184: \tif labelsChanged {\n185: \t\toldServices, err := GetPodServiceMemberships(serviceLister, oldPod)\n186: \t\tif err != nil {\n187: \t\t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"unable to get pod %s/%s's service memberships: %v\", oldPod.Namespace, oldPod.Name, err))\n188: \t\t}\n189: \t\tservices = determineNeededServiceUpdates(oldServices, services, podChanged)\n190: \t}\n191: \n192: \treturn services\n193: }\n194: \n195: // GetPodFromDeleteAction returns a pointer to a pod if one can be derived from\n196: // obj (could be a *v1.Pod, or a DeletionFinalStateUnknown marker item).\n197: func GetPodFromDeleteAction(obj interface{}) *v1.Pod {\n198: \tif pod, ok := obj.(*v1.Pod); ok {\n199: \t\t// Enqueue all the services that the pod used to be a member of.\n200: \t\t// This is the same thing we do when we add a pod.\n201: \t\treturn pod\n202: \t}\n203: \t// If we reached here it means the pod was deleted but its final state is unrecorded.\n204: \ttombstone, ok := obj.(cache.DeletedFinalStateUnknown)\n205: \tif !ok {\n206: \t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"couldn't get object from tombstone %#v\", obj))\n207: \t\treturn nil\n208: \t}\n209: \tpod, ok := tombstone.Obj.(*v1.Pod)\n210: \tif !ok {\n211: \t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"tombstone contained object that is not a Pod: %#v\", obj))\n212: \t\treturn nil\n213: \t}\n214: \treturn pod\n215: }\n216: \n217: func hostNameAndDomainAreEqual(pod1, pod2 *v1.Pod) bool {\n218: \treturn pod1.Spec.Hostname == pod2.Spec.Hostname &&\n219: \t\tpod1.Spec.Subdomain == pod2.Spec.Subdomain\n220: }\n221: \n222: func determineNeededServiceUpdates(oldServices, services sets.String, podChanged bool) sets.String {\n223: \tif podChanged {\n224: \t\t// if the labels and pod changed, all services need to be updated\n225: \t\tservices = services.Union(oldServices)\n226: \t} else {\n227: \t\t// if only the labels changed, services not common to both the new\n228: \t\t// and old service set (the disjuntive union) need to be updated\n229: \t\tservices = services.Difference(oldServices).Union(oldServices.Difference(services))\n230: \t}\n231: \treturn services\n232: }\n233: \n234: // portsInOrder helps sort endpoint ports in a consistent way for hashing.\n235: type portsInOrder []discovery.EndpointPort\n236: \n237: func (sl portsInOrder) Len() int      { return len(sl) }\n238: func (sl portsInOrder) Swap(i, j int) { sl[i], sl[j] = sl[j], sl[i] }\n239: func (sl portsInOrder) Less(i, j int) bool {\n240: \th1 := deepHashObjectToString(sl[i])\n241: \th2 := deepHashObjectToString(sl[j])\n242: \treturn h1 < h2\n243: }\n244: \n245: // EndpointsEqualBeyondHash returns true if endpoints have equal attributes\n246: // but excludes equality checks that would have already been covered with\n247: // endpoint hashing (see hashEndpoint func for more info) and ignores difference\n248: // in ResourceVersion of TargetRef.\n249: func EndpointsEqualBeyondHash(ep1, ep2 *discovery.Endpoint) bool {\n250: \tif stringPtrChanged(ep1.NodeName, ep2.NodeName) {\n251: \t\treturn false\n252: \t}\n253: \n254: \tif stringPtrChanged(ep1.Zone, ep2.Zone) {\n255: \t\treturn false\n256: \t}\n257: \n258: \tif boolPtrChanged(ep1.Conditions.Ready, ep2.Conditions.Ready) {\n259: \t\treturn false\n260: \t}\n261: \n262: \tif boolPtrChanged(ep1.Conditions.Serving, ep2.Conditions.Serving) {\n263: \t\treturn false\n264: \t}\n265: \n266: \tif boolPtrChanged(ep1.Conditions.Terminating, ep2.Conditions.Terminating) {\n267: \t\treturn false\n268: \t}\n269: \n270: \tif !semanticIgnoreResourceVersion.DeepEqual(ep1.TargetRef, ep2.TargetRef) {\n271: \t\treturn false\n272: \t}\n273: \n274: \treturn true\n275: }\n276: \n277: // boolPtrChanged returns true if a set of bool pointers have different values.\n278: func boolPtrChanged(ptr1, ptr2 *bool) bool {\n279: \tif (ptr1 == nil) != (ptr2 == nil) {\n280: \t\treturn true\n281: \t}\n282: \tif ptr1 != nil && ptr2 != nil && *ptr1 != *ptr2 {\n283: \t\treturn true\n284: \t}\n285: \treturn false\n286: }\n287: \n288: // stringPtrChanged returns true if a set of string pointers have different values.\n289: func stringPtrChanged(ptr1, ptr2 *string) bool {\n290: \tif (ptr1 == nil) != (ptr2 == nil) {\n291: \t\treturn true\n292: \t}\n293: \tif ptr1 != nil && ptr2 != nil && *ptr1 != *ptr2 {\n294: \t\treturn true\n295: \t}\n296: \treturn false\n297: }\n298: \n299: // DeepHashObject writes specified object to hash using the spew library\n300: // which follows pointers and prints actual values of the nested objects\n301: // ensuring the hash does not change when a pointer changes.\n302: // copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/util/hash\n303: func deepHashObject(hasher hash.Hash, objectToWrite interface{}) {\n304: \thasher.Reset()\n305: \tprinter := spew.ConfigState{\n306: \t\tIndent:         \" \",\n307: \t\tSortKeys:       true,\n308: \t\tDisableMethods: true,\n309: \t\tSpewKeys:       true,\n310: \t}\n311: \tprinter.Fprintf(hasher, \"%#v\", objectToWrite)\n312: }\n313: \n314: // IsPodReady returns true if Pods Ready condition is true\n315: // copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\n316: func IsPodReady(pod *v1.Pod) bool {\n317: \treturn isPodReadyConditionTrue(pod.Status)\n318: }\n319: \n320: // IsPodTerminal returns true if a pod is terminal, all containers are stopped and cannot ever regress.\n321: // copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\n322: func isPodTerminal(pod *v1.Pod) bool {\n323: \treturn isPodPhaseTerminal(pod.Status.Phase)\n324: }\n325: \n326: // IsPodPhaseTerminal returns true if the pod's phase is terminal.\n327: // copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\n328: func isPodPhaseTerminal(phase v1.PodPhase) bool {\n329: \treturn phase == v1.PodFailed || phase == v1.PodSucceeded\n330: }\n331: \n332: // IsPodReadyConditionTrue returns true if a pod is ready; false otherwise.\n333: // copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\n334: func isPodReadyConditionTrue(status v1.PodStatus) bool {\n335: \tcondition := getPodReadyCondition(&status)\n336: \treturn condition != nil && condition.Status == v1.ConditionTrue\n337: }\n338: \n339: // getPodReadyCondition extracts the pod ready condition from the given status and returns that.\n340: // Returns nil if the condition is not present.\n341: // copied from k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\n342: func getPodReadyCondition(status *v1.PodStatus) *v1.PodCondition {\n343: \tif status == nil || status.Conditions == nil {\n344: \t\treturn nil\n345: \t}\n346: \n347: \tfor i := range status.Conditions {\n348: \t\tif status.Conditions[i].Type == v1.PodReady {\n349: \t\t\treturn &status.Conditions[i]\n350: \t\t}\n351: \t}\n352: \treturn nil\n353: }\n354: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/util/controller_utils.go"
  }

([#96600](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/96600), [@maplain](https://github.com/maplain)) [SIG API Machinery, Apps and Network]\\n2452: \",\"endLine\":2452,\"startLine\":2447},{\"content\":\"1820: - One new field \\\"InternalTrafficPolicy\\\" in Service is added.\\n1821:   It specifies if the cluster internal traffic should be routed to all endpoints or node-local endpoints only.\\n1822:   \\\"Cluster\\\" routes internal traffic to a Service to all endpoints.\\n1823:   \\\"Local\\\" routes traffic to node-local endpoints only, and traffic is dropped if no node-local endpoints are ready.\\n1824:   The default value is \\\"Cluster\\\". ([#96600](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/96600), [@maplain](https://github.com/maplain)) [SIG API Machinery, Apps and Network]\\n1825: \",\"endLine\":1825,\"startLine\":1820},{\"content\":\"3106:   - Make `build-in-docker` and `test-in-docker` work on Linux systems with SELinux enabled\\n3107:   - Fix an error where existing nodes were not considered as destinations while finding place for pods in scale-down simulations\\n3108:   - Remove redundant log lines and reduce severity around parsing kubeEnv\\n3109:   - Don't treat nodes created by virtual kubelet as nodes from non-autoscaled node groups\\n3110:   - Remove redundant logging around calculating node utilization\\n3111:   - Add configurable `--network` and `--rm` flags for docker in `Makefile`\\n3112:   - Subtract DaemonSet pods' requests from node allocatable in the denominator while computing node utilization\\n3113:   - Include taints by condition when determining if a node is unready/still starting\\n3114:   - Fix `update-vendor.sh` to work on OSX and zsh\\n3115:   - Add best-effort eviction for DaemonSet pods while scaling down non-empty nodes\\n3116:   - Add build support for ARM64\\n3117: \",\"endLine\":3117,\"startLine\":3106}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.21.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.21.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"1990:   handle it for them.  Services are single-stack unless the user asks for\\n1991:   dual-stack.  This is all gated by the \\\"IPv6DualStack\\\" feature gate. ([#91824](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/91824), [@khenidak](https://github.com/khenidak)) [SIG API Machinery, Apps, CLI, Network, Node, Scheduling and Testing]\\n1992: - Add support for hugepages to downward API ([#86102](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/86102), [@derekwaynecarr](https://github.com/derekwaynecarr)) [SIG API Machinery, Apps, CLI, Network, Node, Scheduling and Testing]\\n1993: - Adds kubelet alpha feature, `GracefulNodeShutdown` which makes kubelet aware of node system shutdowns and result in graceful termination of pods during a system shutdown. ([#96129](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/96129), [@bobbypage](https: ... [line truncated]\\n1994: ... [chunk truncated]\",\"endLine\":2005,\"startLine\":1990},{\"content\":\"2665: - Add LoadBalancerIPMode feature gate ([#92312](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/92312), [@Sh4d1](https://github.com/Sh4d1)) [SIG Apps, CLI, Cloud Provider and Network]\\n2666: - Add WindowsContainerResources and Annotations to CRI-API UpdateContainerResourcesRequest ([#95741](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/95741), [@katiewasnothere](https://github.com/katiewasnothere)) [SIG Node]\\n2667: - Add a 'serving' and `terminating` condition to the EndpointSlice API.\\n2668:   \\n2669:   `serving` tracks the readiness of endpoints regardless of their terminating state. This is distinct from `ready` since `ready` is only true when pods are not terminating. \\n2670:   `terminating` is true when an endpoint is terminating. For pods this is any endpoint with a deletion timestamp. ([#92968](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/92968), [@andrewsykim](https://github.com/andrewsykim)) [SIG Apps and Network]\\n2671: ... [chunk truncated]\",\"endLine\":2681,\"startLine\":2665},{\"content\":\"1252:   - Make `build-in-docker` and `test-in-docker` work on Linux systems with SELinux enabled\\n1253:   - Fix an error where existing nodes were not considered as destinations while finding place for pods in scale-down simulations\\n1254:   - Remove redundant log lines and reduce severity around parsing kubeEnv\\n1255:   - Don't treat nodes created by virtual kubelet as nodes from non-autoscaled node groups\\n1256:   - Remove redundant logging around calculating node utilization\\n1257:   - Add configurable `--network` and `--rm` flags for docker in `Makefile`\\n1258:   - Subtract DaemonSet pods' requests from node allocatable in the denominator while computing node utilization\\n1259:   - Include taints by condition when determining if a node is unready/still starting\\n1260:   - Fix `update-vendor.sh` to work on OSX and zsh\\n1261:   - Add best-effort eviction for DaemonSet pods while scaling down non-empty nodes\\n1262:   - Add build support for ARM64\\n1263: \",\"endLine\":1263,\"startLine\":1252}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"1306: \\n1307: * **Taints**\\n1308:   * You will need to use `kubectl taint` to re-create all of your taints after kubectl and the master are upgraded to 1.6. Between the time the master is upgraded to 1.6 and when you do this, your existing taints will have no effect.\\n1309:   * You can find out what taints you have in place on a node while you are still running Kubernetes 1.5 by doing `kubectl describe node <node name>`; the `Taints` section will show the taints you have in place. To see the taints that were created under 1.5 ... [line truncated]\\n1310:   * You can remove the \\\"old\\\" taints (stored internally as annotations) at any time after the upgrade by doing `kubectl annotate nodes <node name> scheduler.alpha.kubernetes.io/taints-` (note the minus at the end, which means \\\"delete the taint with this key ... [line truncated]\\n1311: ... Use ScorePlugin only. ([#80930](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/80930), [@liu-cong](https://github.com/liu-cong))\\n1634:   - the node label `alpha.service-controller.kubernetes.io/exclude-balancer` which excludes a node from cloud load balancers (using Service Type=LoadBalancer) is deprecated in favor of `node.kubernetes.io/exclude-balancer`. Support for `alpha.service-contr ... [line truncated]\\n1635: \\n1636: \",\"endLine\":1636,\"startLine\":1633},{\"content\":\"1936: \\n1937: ### Node\\n1938: \\n1939: \",\"endLine\":1939,\"startLine\":1936},{\"content\":\"916: \\n917: ### Node Binaries\\n918: \\n919: \",\"endLine\":919,\"startLine\":916}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.16.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.16.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"2470: - Docker runtime support using dockershim in the kubelet is now completely removed in 1.24. The kubelet used to have a module called dockershim, which implements CRI support for Docker, and it has seen maintenance issues in the Kubernetes community. From 1 ... [line truncated]\\n2471: - Fixed bug with leads to Node goes `Not-ready` state when credentials for vCenter stored in a secret and Zones feature is in use. Zone labels setup moved to KCM component, kubelet skips this step during startup in such case. If credentials stored in cloud ... [line truncated]\\n2472: - The `LegacyServiceAccountTokenNoAutoGeneration` feature gate is beta, and enabled by default. When enabled, Secret API objects containing service account tokens are no longer auto-generated for every ServiceAccount. Use the [TokenRequest](https://kuberne ... [line truncated]\\n2473: - The calculations for Pod topology spread skew now exclude nodes that\\n2474: ... [chunk truncated]\",\"endLine\":2482,\"startLine\":2470},{\"content\":\"3821: \\n3822:  - Docker runtime support using dockshim in the kubelet is now completely removed in 1.24. The kubelet used to have a a module called \\\"dockershim\\\" which implements CRI support for Docker and it has seen maintenance issues in the Kubernetes community. From  ... [line truncated]\\n3823:   - The calculations for Pod topology spread skew now excludes nodes that\\n3824:   don't match the node affinity/selector. This may lead to unschedulable pods if you previously had pods\\n3825:   matching the spreading selector on those excluded nodes (not matching the node affinity/selector),\\n3826:   especially when the topologyKey is not node-level. Revisit the node affinity and/or pod selector in the\\n3827:   topology spread constraints to avoid this scenario. ([#107009](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/107009), [@kerthcet](https://github.com/kerthcet)) [SIG Scheduling]\\n3828: \",\"endLine\":3828,\"startLine\":3821},{\"content\":\"1712: \\n1713: ### Node Binaries\\n1714: \\n1715: \",\"endLine\":1715,\"startLine\":1712}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.24.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.24.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"1039: \\n1040: ### SIG-node\\n1041: \\n1042: SIG-node graduated the PodShareProcessNamespace feature from alpha to beta.  This feature allows a pod spec to request that all containers in a pod share a common process namespaces.\\n1043: \\n1044: \",\"endLine\":1044,\"startLine\":1039},{\"content\":\"1333: \\n1334: ### SIG Node\\n1335: \\n1336: \",\"endLine\":1336,\"startLine\":1333},{\"content\":\"267: \\n268: ### Node Binaries\\n269: \\n270: \",\"endLine\":270,\"startLine\":267}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.12.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.12.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"627: \\n628: ### Node Binaries\\n629: \\n630: \",\"endLine\":630,\"startLine\":627},{\"content\":\"523: \\n524: ### Node Binaries\\n525: \\n526: \",\"endLine\":526,\"startLine\":523},{\"content\":\"323: \\n324: ### Node Binaries\\n325: \\n326: \",\"endLine\":326,\"startLine\":323}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.28.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.28.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"1040: \\n1041: In addition, the NodeRestriction admission plugin now prevents kubelets from modifying taints on their Node API objects, making it easier to keep track of which nodes should be in use.\\n1042: \\n1043: \",\"endLine\":1043,\"startLine\":1040},{\"content\":\"2509:     *   \\\"loadBalancerSku\\\": \\\"standard\\\",\\n2510:     *   \\\"excludeMasterFromStandardLB\\\": true,\\n2511:     * }\\n2512:     * If excludeMasterFromStandardLB is not set, it will be default to true, which means master nodes are excluded to the backend of standard LB.\\n2513:     * Also note standard load balancer doesn't work with annotation `service.beta.kubernetes.io/azure-load-balancer-mode`. This is because all nodes (except master) are added as the LB backends.\\n2514: * The node authorizer now automatically sets up rules for Node.Spec.ConfigSource when the DynamicKubeletConfig feature gate is enabled. ([#60100](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/60100), [@mtaufen](https://github.com/mtaufen))\\n2515: * Update kube-dns to Version 1.14.9. Major changes: ([#61908](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/61908), [@MrHohn](https://github.com/MrHohn))\\n2516: \",\"endLine\":2516,\"startLine\":2509},{\"content\":\"1066: \\n1067: ### SIG Node\\n1068: \\n1069: SIG-Node advanced several features and made incremental improvements in a few key topic areas this release.\\n1070: \\n1071: The dynamic kubelet config feature graduated to beta, so it is enabled by default, simplifying management of the node object itself.  Kubelets that are configured to work with the CRI may take advantage of the log rotation feature, which is graduating to b ... [line truncated]\\n1072: \\n1073: The cri-tools project, which aims to provide consistent tooling for operators to debug and introspect their nodes in production independent of their chosen container runtime, graduated to GA.\\n1074: \\n1075: \",\"endLine\":1075,\"startLine\":1066}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"3364: \\n3365: - EndpointSlices with Pod referencing Nodes that doesn't exist couldn't be created or updated.\\n3366:   The behavior on the EndpointSlice controller has been modified to update the EndpointSlice without the Pods that reference non-existing Nodes, and keep retrying until all Pods reference existing Nodes.\\n3367:   However, if service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses is set, all the Pods are published without retrying.\\n3368:   Fixed EndpointSlices metrics to reflect correctly the number of desired EndpointSlices when no endpoints are present. ([#110639](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110639), [@aojea](https://github.com/aojea)) [SIG Apps and Network]\\n3369: \\n3370: \",\"endLine\":3370,\"startLine\":3364},{\"content\":\"1249: \\n1250: ### Node Binaries\\n1251: \\n1252: \",\"endLine\":1252,\"startLine\":1249},{\"content\":\"2084: \\n2085: ### Node Binaries\\n2086: \\n2087: \",\"endLine\":2087,\"startLine\":2084}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"1151: \\n1152: ### Node\\n1153: \\n1154: SIG Node iterated on the ability to support more workloads with better performance and improved reliability.  Alpha features were improved around hardware accelerator support, device plugins enablement, and cpu pinning policies to enable us to graduate the ... [line truncated]\\n1155: \\n1156: \",\"endLine\":1156,\"startLine\":1151},{\"content\":\"1583: \\n1584: ### **Node**\\n1585: \\n1586: \",\"endLine\":1586,\"startLine\":1583},{\"content\":\"1310: \\n1311: ### **Node**\\n1312: \\n1313: \\n1399: ### SIG Node\\n1400: \\n1401: [SIG Node][] is responsible for the components that support the controlled\\n1402: interactions between pods and host resources, and manage the lifecycle\\n1403: of pods scheduled on a node.\\n1404: \\n1405: For the 1.8 release, SIG Node continued to focus\\n1406: on a broad set of workload types, including hardware and performance\\n1407: \",\"endLine\":1407,\"startLine\":1398},{\"content\":\"786: \\n787: ### Node Binaries\\n788: \\n789: \",\"endLine\":789,\"startLine\":786},{\"content\":\"849: \\n850: ### Node Binaries\\n851: \\n852: \",\"endLine\":852,\"startLine\":849}],\"file\":\"CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.8.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.8.md\"}]}",

        "file": "api/api-rules/violation_exceptions.list",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",

      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "2447: - One new field \"InternalTrafficPolicy\" in Service is added.\n2448:   It specifies if the cluster internal traffic should be routed to all endpoints or node-local endpoints only.\n2449:   \"Cluster\" routes internal traffic to a Service to all endpoints.\n2450:   \"Local\" routes traffic to node-local endpoints only, and traffic is dropped if no node-local endpoints are ready.\n2451:   The default value is \"Cluster\". ([#96600](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/96600), [@maplain](https://github.com/maplain)) [SIG API Machinery, Apps and Network]\n2452: ",
            "endLine": 2452,
            "startLine": 2447
          },
          {
            "content": "1820: - One new field \"InternalTrafficPolicy\" in Service is added.\n1821:   It specifies if the cluster internal traffic should be routed to all endpoints or node-local endpoints only.\n1822:   \"Cluster\" routes internal traffic to a Service to all endpoints.\n1823:   \"Local\" routes traffic to node-local endpoints only, and traffic is dropped if no node-local endpoints are ready.\n1824:   The default value is \"Cluster\". ([#96600](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/96600), [@maplain](https://github.com/maplain)) [SIG API Machinery, Apps and Network]\n1825: ",
            "endLine": 1825,
            "startLine": 1820
          },
          {
            "content": "3106:   - Make `build-in-docker` and `test-in-docker` work on Linux systems with SELinux enabled\n3107:   - Fix an error where existing nodes were not considered as destinations while finding place for pods in scale-down simulations\n3108:   - Remove redundant log lines and reduce severity around parsing kubeEnv\n3109:   - Don't treat nodes created by virtual kubelet as nodes from non-autoscaled node groups\n3110:   - Remove redundant logging around calculating node utilization\n3111:   - Add configurable `--network` and `--rm` flags for docker in `Makefile`\n3112:   - Subtract DaemonSet pods' requests from node allocatable in the denominator while computing node utilization\n3113:   - Include taints by condition when determining if a node is unready/still starting\n3114:   - Fix `update-vendor.sh` to work on OSX and zsh\n3115:   - Add best-effort eviction for DaemonSet pods while scaling down non-empty nodes\n3116:   - Add build support for ARM64\n3117: ",
            "endLine": 3117,
            "startLine": 3106
          }
        ],
        "file": "CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.21.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.21.md"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "1990:   handle it for them.  Services are single-stack unless the user asks for\n1991:   dual-stack.  This is all gated by the \"IPv6DualStack\" feature gate. ([#91824](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/91824), [@khenidak](https://github.com/khenidak)) [SIG API Machinery, Apps, CLI, Network, Node, Scheduling and Testing]\n1992: - Add support for hugepages to downward API ([#86102](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/86102), [@derekwaynecarr](https://github.com/derekwaynecarr)) [SIG API Machinery, Apps, CLI, Network, Node, Scheduling and Testing]\n1993: - Adds kubelet alpha feature, `GracefulNodeShutdown` which makes kubelet aware of node system shutdowns and result in graceful termination of pods during a system shutdown. ([#96129](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/96129), [@bobbypage](https: ... [line truncated]\n1994: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 2005,
            "startLine": 1990
          },
          {
            "content": "2665: - Add LoadBalancerIPMode feature gate ([#92312](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/92312), [@Sh4d1](https://github.com/Sh4d1)) [SIG Apps, CLI, Cloud Provider and Network]\n2666: - Add WindowsContainerResources and Annotations to CRI-API UpdateContainerResourcesRequest ([#95741](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/95741), [@katiewasnothere](https://github.com/katiewasnothere)) [SIG Node]\n2667: - Add a 'serving' and `terminating` condition to the EndpointSlice API.\n2668:   \n2669:   `serving` tracks the readiness of endpoints regardless of their terminating state. This is distinct from `ready` since `ready` is only true when pods are not terminating. \n2670:   `terminating` is true when an endpoint is terminating. For pods this is any endpoint with a deletion timestamp. ([#92968](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/92968), [@andrewsykim](https://github.com/andrewsykim)) [SIG Apps and Network]\n2671: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 2681,
            "startLine": 2665
          },
          {
            "content": "1252:   - Make `build-in-docker` and `test-in-docker` work on Linux systems with SELinux enabled\n1253:   - Fix an error where existing nodes were not considered as destinations while finding place for pods in scale-down simulations\n1254:   - Remove redundant log lines and reduce severity around parsing kubeEnv\n1255:   - Don't treat nodes created by virtual kubelet as nodes from non-autoscaled node groups\n1256:   - Remove redundant logging around calculating node utilization\n1257:   - Add configurable `--network` and `--rm` flags for docker in `Makefile`\n1258:   - Subtract DaemonSet pods' requests from node allocatable in the denominator while computing node utilization\n1259:   - Include taints by condition when determining if a node is unready/still starting\n1260:   - Fix `update-vendor.sh` to work on OSX and zsh\n1261:   - Add best-effort eviction for DaemonSet pods while scaling down non-empty nodes\n1262:   - Add build support for ARM64\n1263: ",
            "endLine": 1263,
            "startLine": 1252
          }
        ],

        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "1306: \n1307: * **Taints**\n1308:   * You will need to use `kubectl taint` to re-create all of your taints after kubectl and the master are upgraded to 1.6. Between the time the master is upgraded to 1.6 and when you do this, your existing taints will have no effect.\n1309:   * You can find out what taints you have in place on a node while you are still running Kubernetes 1.5 by doing `kubectl describe node <node name>`; the `Taints` section will show the taints you have in place. To see the taints that were created under 1.5 ... [line truncated]\n1310:   * You can remove the \"old\" taints (stored internally as annotations) at any time after the upgrade by doing `kubectl annotate nodes <node name> scheduler.alpha.kubernetes.io/taints-` (note the minus at the end, which means \"delete the taint with this key ... [line truncated]\n1311: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 1314,
            "startLine": 1306
          },
          {

            "endLine": 1500,

      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "2470: - Docker runtime support using dockershim in the kubelet is now completely removed in 1.24. The kubelet used to have a module called dockershim, which implements CRI support for Docker, and it has seen maintenance issues in the Kubernetes community. From 1 ... [line truncated]\n2471: - Fixed bug with leads to Node goes `Not-ready` state when credentials for vCenter stored in a secret and Zones feature is in use. Zone labels setup moved to KCM component, kubelet skips this step during startup in such case. If credentials stored in cloud ... [line truncated]\n2472: - The `LegacyServiceAccountTokenNoAutoGeneration` feature gate is beta, and enabled by default. When enabled, Secret API objects containing service account tokens are no longer auto-generated for every ServiceAccount. Use the [TokenRequest](https://kuberne ... [line truncated]\n2473: - The calculations for Pod topology spread skew now exclude nodes that\n2474: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 2482,
            "startLine": 2470
          },
          {
            "content": "3821: \n3822:  - Docker runtime support using dockshim in the kubelet is now completely removed in 1.24. The kubelet used to have a a module called \"dockershim\" which implements CRI support for Docker and it has seen maintenance issues in the Kubernetes community. From  ... [line truncated]\n3823:   - The calculations for Pod topology spread skew now excludes nodes that\n3824:   don't match the node affinity/selector. This may lead to unschedulable pods if you previously had pods\n3825:   matching the spreading selector on those excluded nodes (not matching the node affinity/selector),\n3826:   especially when the topologyKey is not node-level. Revisit the node affinity and/or pod selector in the\n3827:   topology spread constraints to avoid this scenario. ([#107009](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/107009), [@kerthcet](https://github.com/kerthcet)) [SIG Scheduling]\n3828: ",

          {
            "content": "523: \n524: ### Node Binaries\n525: \n526: ",
            "endLine": 526,
            "startLine": 523
          },
          {
            "content": "323: \n324: ### Node Binaries\n325: \n326: ",
            "endLine": 326,
            "startLine": 323
          }
        ],
        "file": "CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.28.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.28.md"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "1040: \n1041: In addition, the NodeRestriction admission plugin now prevents kubelets from modifying taints on their Node API objects, making it easier to keep track of which nodes should be in use.\n1042: \n1043: ",
            "endLine": 1043,
            "startLine": 1040
          },
          {
            "content": "2509:     *   \"loadBalancerSku\": \"standard\",\n2510:     *   \"excludeMasterFromStandardLB\": true,\n2511:     * }\n2512:     * If excludeMasterFromStandardLB is not set, it will be default to true, which means master nodes are excluded to the backend of standard LB.\n2513:     * Also note standard load balancer doesn't work with annotation `service.beta.kubernetes.io/azure-load-balancer-mode`. This is because all nodes (except master) are added as the LB backends.\n2514: * The node authorizer now automatically sets up rules for Node.Spec.ConfigSource when the DynamicKubeletConfig feature gate is enabled. ([#60100](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/60100), [@mtaufen](https://github.com/mtaufen))\n2515: * Update kube-dns to Version 1.14.9. Major changes: ([#61908](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/61908), [@MrHohn](https://github.com/MrHohn))\n2516: ",
            "endLine": 2516,
            "startLine": 2509
          },
          {
            "content": "1066: \n1067: ### SIG Node\n1068: \n1069: SIG-Node advanced several features and made incremental improvements in a few key topic areas this release.\n1070: \n1071: The dynamic kubelet config feature graduated to beta, so it is enabled by default, simplifying management of the node object itself.  Kubelets that are configured to work with the CRI may take advantage of the log rotation feature, which is graduating to b ... [line truncated]\n1072: \n1073: The cri-tools project, which aims to provide consistent tooling for operators to debug and introspect their nodes in production independent of their chosen container runtime, graduated to GA.\n1074: \n1075: ",
            "endLine": 1075,
            "startLine": 1066
          }
        ],
        "file": "CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "3364: \n3365: - EndpointSlices with Pod referencing Nodes that doesn't exist couldn't be created or updated.\n3366:   The behavior on the EndpointSlice controller has been modified to update the EndpointSlice without the Pods that reference non-existing Nodes, and keep retrying until all Pods reference existing Nodes.\n3367:   However, if service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses is set, all the Pods are published without retrying.\n3368:   Fixed EndpointSlices metrics to reflect correctly the number of desired EndpointSlices when no endpoints are present. ([#110639](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110639), [@aojea](https://github.com/aojea)) [SIG Apps and Network]\n3369: \n3370: ",
            "endLine": 3370,
            "startLine": 3364
          },
          {
            "content": "1249: \n1250: ### Node Binaries\n1251: \n1252: ",
            "endLine": 1252,
            "startLine": 1249
          },
          {
            "content": "2084: \n2085: ### Node Binaries\n2086: \n2087: ",
            "endLine": 2087,
            "startLine": 2084
          }
        ],
        "file": "CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0/-/blob/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "1151: \n1152: ### Node\n1153: \n1154: SIG Node iterated on the ability to support more workloads with better performance and improved reliability.  Alpha features were improved around hardware accelerator support, device plugins enablement, and cpu pinning policies to enable us to graduate the ... [line truncated]\n1155: \n1156: ",
            "endLine": 1156,
            "startLine": 1151

        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "1398: \n1399: ### SIG Node\n1400: \n1401: [SIG Node][] is responsible for the components that support the controlled\n1402: interactions between pods and host resources, and manage the lifecycle\n1403: of pods scheduled on a node.\n1404: \n1405: For the 1.8 release, SIG Node continued to focus\n1406: on a broad set of workload types, including hardware and performance\n1407: ",
            "endLine": 1407,

    "content": "3160: \tTimeAdded *metav1.Time\n3161: }\n3162: \n3163: // TaintEffect defines the effects of Taint\n3164: type TaintEffect string\n3165: \n3166: // These are valid values for TaintEffect\n3167: const (\n3168: \t// Do not allow new pods to schedule onto the node unless they tolerate the taint,\n3169: \t// but allow all pods submitted to Kubelet without going through the scheduler\n3170: \t// to start, and allow all already-running pods to continue running.\n3171: \t// Enforced by the scheduler.\n3172: \tTaintEffectNoSchedule TaintEffect = \"NoSchedule\"\n3173: \t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but the scheduler tries not to schedule\n3174: \t// new pods onto the node, rather than prohibiting new pods from scheduling\n3175: \t// onto the node entirely. Enforced by the scheduler.\\n3176: \\tTaintEffectPreferNoSchedule TaintEffect = \\\"PreferNoSchedule\\\"\\n3177: \\t// NOT YET IMPLEMENTED. TODO: Uncomment field once it is implemented.\\n3178: \\t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally do not allow pods submitted to\\n3179: \\t// Kubelet without going through the scheduler to start.\\n3180: \\t// Enforced by Kubelet and the scheduler.\\n3181: \\t// TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit TaintEffect = \\\"NoScheduleNoAdmit\\\"\\n3182: \\n3183: \\t// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\\n3184: \\t// Currently enforced by NodeController.\\n3185: \\tTaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \\\"NoExecute\\\"\\n3186: )\\n3187: \\n3188: // Toleration represents the toleration object that can be attached to a pod.\\n3189: // The pod this Toleration is attached to tolerates any taint that matches\\n3190: // the triple <key,value,effect> using the matching operator <operator>.\\n3191: type Toleration struct {\\n3192: \\t// Key is the taint key that the toleration applies to. Empty means match all taint keys.\\n3193: \\t// If the key is empty, operator must be Exists; this combination means to match all values and all keys.\\n3194: \\t// +optional\\n3195: \\tKey string\\n3196: \\t// Operator represents a key's relationship to the value.\\n3197: \\t// Valid operators are Exists and Equal. Defaults to Equal.\\n3198: \\t// Exists is equivalent to wildcard for value, so that a pod can\\n3199: \\t// tolerate all taints of a particular category.\\n3200: \\t// +optional\\n3201: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/pkg/apis/core/types.go%3FL3155-3200\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "3155: \t// Valid effects are NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule and NoExecute.\n3156: \tEffect TaintEffect\n3157: \t// TimeAdded represents the time at which the taint was added.\n3158: \t// It is only written for NoExecute taints.\n3159: \t// +optional\n3160: \tTimeAdded *metav1.Time\n3161: }\n3162: \n3163: // TaintEffect defines the effects of Taint\n3164: type TaintEffect string\n3165: \n3166: // These are valid values for TaintEffect\n3167: const (\n3168: \t// Do not allow new pods to schedule onto the node unless they tolerate the taint,\n3169: \t// but allow all pods submitted to Kubelet without going through the scheduler\n3170: \t// to start, and allow all already-running pods to continue running.\n3171: \t// Enforced by the scheduler.\n3172: \tTaintEffectNoSchedule TaintEffect = \"NoSchedule\"\n3173: \t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but the scheduler tries not to schedule\n3174: \t// new pods onto the node, rather than prohibiting new pods from scheduling\n3175: \t// onto the node entirely. Enforced by the scheduler.\n3176: \tTaintEffectPreferNoSchedule TaintEffect = \"PreferNoSchedule\"\n3177: \t// NOT YET IMPLEMENTED. TODO: Uncomment field once it is implemented.\n3178: \t// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally do not allow pods submitted to\n3179: \t// Kubelet without going through the scheduler to start.\n3180: \t// Enforced by Kubelet and the scheduler.\n3181: \t// TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoAdmit\"\n3182: \n3183: \t// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\n3184: \t// Currently enforced by NodeController.\n3185: \tTaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \"NoExecute\"\n3186: )\n3187: \n3188: // Toleration represents the toleration object that can be attached to a pod.\n3189: // The pod this Toleration is attached to tolerates any taint that matches\n3190: // the triple <key,value,effect> using the matching operator <operator>.\n3191: type Toleration struct {\n3192: \t// Key is the taint key that the toleration applies to. Empty means match all taint keys.\n3193: \t// If the key is empty, operator must be Exists; this combination means to match all values and all keys.\n3194: \t// +optional\n3195: \tKey string\n3196: \t// Operator represents a key's relationship to the value.\n3197: \t// Valid operators are Exists and Equal. Defaults to Equal.\n3198: \t// Exists is equivalent to wildcard for value, so that a pod can\n3199: \t// tolerate all taints of a particular category.\n3200: \t// +optional\n3201: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/pkg/apis/core/types.go%3FL3155-3200"
  }

37. Agent @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Implement NoScheduleNoTraffic taint effect",
  "prompt": "I'm implementing a new Kubernetes taint effect called \"NoScheduleNoTraffic\". You need to modify source files in the kubernetes--v1.30.0 repository to add support for this new effect. This taint effect should:\n- Prevent new pods from being scheduled (like NoSchedule)\n- Exclude the node from Service EndpointSlices (affects traffic routing)\n- NOT evict existing pods (unlike NoExecute)\n\nHere are the files that need modification:\n\n1. **pkg/apis/core/types.go** (line ~3177): Add the constant definition after PreferNoSchedule:\n   ```\n   // Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally remove the node from Service \n   // EndpointSlices, preventing traffic from being routed to the node.\n   // Enforced by the scheduler and endpoint slice controller.\n   TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoTraffic\"\n   ```\n\n2. **staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go** (line ~3519): Add the same constant definition\n\n3. **pkg/util/taints/taints.go** (line ~82): Update validateTaintEffect function to accept NoScheduleNoTraffic:\n   Change: `if effect != v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule && effect != v1.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule && effect != v1.TaintEffectNoExecute`\n   To: `if effect != v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule && effect != v1.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule && effect != v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic && effect != v1.TaintEffectNoExecute`\n\n4. **staging/src/k8s.io/kubectl/pkg/cmd/taint/utils.go** (line ~121): Same change as #3\n\n5. list)\n\n6. **pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/helper/taint.go** (line ~26): Update DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc to include NoScheduleNoTraffic:\n   Change: `return t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute`\n   To: `return t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute`\n\n7. **staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go** (around line ~188-212): Modify the reconciliation logic to skip pods on nodes with NoScheduleNoTraffic taints:\n   After getting the node (line 188), add a check to skip this pod if the node has a NoScheduleNoTraffic taint and PublishNotReadyAddresses is false\n\n8. **test/e2e/framework/node/resource.go** (line ~422): Update toleratesTaintsWithNoScheduleNoExecuteEffects function:\n   Change: `if taint.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute || taint.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule`\n   To: `if taint.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute || taint.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule || taint.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic`\n\nYour task:\n1.   "prompt": "I'm implementing a new Kubernetes taint effect called \"NoScheduleNoTraffic\". You need to modify source files in the kubernetes--v1.30.0 repository to add support for this new effect. This taint effect should:\n- Prevent new pods from being scheduled (like NoSchedule)\n- Exclude the node from Service EndpointSlices (affects traffic routing)\n- NOT evict existing pods (unlike NoExecute)\n\nHere are the files that need modification:\n\n1. **pkg/apis/core/types.go** (line ~3177): Add the constant definition after PreferNoSchedule:\n   ```\n   // Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally remove the node from Service \n   // EndpointSlices, preventing traffic from being routed to the node.\n   // Enforced by the scheduler and endpoint slice controller.\n 53. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "// File: pkg/apis/core/types.go (lines 3170-3186)\n// Context around the TaintEffect constants\n\n// to start, and allow all already-running pods to continue running.\n// Enforced by the scheduler.\nTaintEffectNoSchedule TaintEffect = \"NoSchedule\"\n// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but the scheduler tries not to schedule\n// new pods onto the node, rather than prohibiting new pods from scheduling\n// onto the node entirely. Enforced by the scheduler.\nTaintEffectPreferNoSchedule TaintEffect = \"PreferNoSchedule\"\n// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally remove the node from Service\n// EndpointSlices, preventing traffic from being routed to the node.\n// Enforced by the scheduler and endpoint slice controller.\nTaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoTraffic\"\n// NOT YET IMPLEMENTED. TODO: Uncomment field once it is implemented.\n// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally do not allow pods submitted to\n// Kubelet without going through the scheduler to start.\n// Enforced by Kubelet and the scheduler.\n// TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoAdmit\"\n\n// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\n// Currently enforced by NodeController.\nTaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \"NoExecute\"\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/types_1.go"
}
Output
"File created successfully at: /workspace/types_1.go"
54. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "// File: staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go (lines 3510-3528)\n// Context around the TaintEffect constants\n\n// Do not allow new pods to schedule onto the node unless they tolerate the taint,\n// but allow all pods submitted to Kubelet without going through the scheduler\n// to start, and allow all already-running pods to continue running.\n// Enforced by the scheduler.\nTaintEffectNoSchedule TaintEffect = \"NoSchedule\"\n// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but the scheduler tries not to schedule\n// new pods onto the node, rather than prohibiting new pods from scheduling\n// onto the node entirely. Enforced by the scheduler.\nTaintEffectPreferNoSchedule TaintEffect = \"PreferNoSchedule\"\n// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally remove the node from Service\n// EndpointSlices, preventing traffic from being routed to the node.\n// Enforced by the scheduler and endpoint slice controller.\nTaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoTraffic\"\n// NOT YET IMPLEMENTED. TODO: Uncomment field once it is implemented.\n// Like TaintEffectNoSchedule, but additionally do not allow pods submitted to\n// Kubelet without going through the scheduler to start.\n// Enforced by Kubelet and the scheduler.\n// TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit TaintEffect = \"NoScheduleNoAdmit\"\n\n// Evict any already-running pods that do not tolerate the taint.\n// Currently enforced by NodeController.\nTaintEffectNoExecute TaintEffect = \"NoExecute\"\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/types_2.go"
}
Output
"File created successfully at: /workspace/types_2.go"
55. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "// File: pkg/util/taints/taints.go (lines 81-87)\n// Updated validateTaintEffect function\n\nfunc validateTaintEffect(effect v1.TaintEffect) error {\n\tif effect != v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule && effect != v1.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule && effect != v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic && effect != v1.TaintEffectNoExecute {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint effect: %v, unsupported taint effect\", effect)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/taints.go"
}
Output
"File created successfully at: /workspace/taints.go"
56. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "// File: staging/src/k8s.io/kubectl/pkg/cmd/taint/utils.go (lines 120-126)\n// Updated validateTaintEffect function\n\nfunc validateTaintEffect(effect corev1.TaintEffect) error {\n\tif effect != corev1.TaintEffectNoSchedule && effect != corev1.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule && effect != corev1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic && effect != corev1.TaintEffectNoExecute {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid taint effect: %v, unsupported taint effect\", effect)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/utils.go"
}
Output
"File created successfully at: /workspace/utils.go"
57. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "// File: pkg/apis/core/validation/validation.go (lines 3858-3879)\n// Updated validateTaintEffect function\n\nfunc validateTaintEffect(effect *core.TaintEffect, allowEmpty bool, fldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {\n\tif !allowEmpty && len(*effect) == 0 {\n\t\treturn field.ErrorList{field.Required(fldPath, \"\")}\n\t}\n\n\tallErrors := field.ErrorList{}\n\tswitch *effect {\n\t// TODO: Replace next line with subsequent commented-out line when implement TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit.\n\tcase core.TaintEffectNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic, core.TaintEffectNoExecute:\n\t\t// case core.TaintEffectNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule, core.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit, core.TaintEffectNoExecute:\n\tdefault:\n\t\tvalidValues := []core.TaintEffect{\n\t\t\tcore.TaintEffectNoSchedule,\n\t\t\tcore.TaintEffectPreferNoSchedule,\n\t\t\tcore.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic,\n\t\t\tcore.TaintEffectNoExecute,\n\t\t\t// TODO: Uncomment this block when implement TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit.\n\t\t\t// core.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoAdmit,\n\t\t}\n\t\tallErrors = append(allErrors, field.NotSupported(fldPath, *effect, validValues))\n\t}\n\treturn allErrors\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/validation.go"
}
Output
"File created successfully at: /workspace/validation.go"
58. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "// File: pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/helper/taint.go (lines 21-28)\n// Updated DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc to include NoScheduleNoTraffic\n\n// DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc returns the filter function that can\n// filter out the node taints that reject scheduling Pod on a Node.\nfunc DoNotScheduleTaintsFilterFunc() func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\n\treturn func(t *v1.Taint) bool {\n\t\t// PodToleratesNodeTaints is interested in NoSchedule, NoScheduleNoTraffic, and NoExecute taints.\n\t\treturn t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic || t.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute\n\t}\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/taint.go"
}
Output
"File created successfully at: /workspace/taint.go"
59. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "// File: staging/src/k8s.io/endpointslice/reconciler.go (lines 180-212)\n// Enhanced reconciliation logic to skip pods on nodes with NoScheduleNoTraffic taints\n\n\t\tnode, err := r.nodeLister.Get(pod.Spec.NodeName)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t// we are getting the information from the local informer,\n\t\t\t// an error different than IsNotFound should not happen\n\t\t\tif !errors.IsNotFound(err) {\n\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// If the Node specified by the Pod doesn't exist we want to requeue the Service so we\n\t\t\t// retry later, but also update the EndpointSlice without the problematic Pod.\n\t\t\t// Theoretically, the pod Garbage Collector will remove the Pod, but we want to avoid\n\t\t\t// situations where a reference from a Pod to a missing node can leave the EndpointSlice\n\t\t\t// stuck forever.\n\t\t\t// On the other side, if the service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses is set we just add the\n\t\t\t// Pod, since the user is explicitly indicating that the Pod address should be published.\n\t\t\tif !service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses {\n\t\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"skipping Pod for Service, Node not found\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"service\", klog.KObj(service), \"node\", klog.KRef(\"\", pod.Spec.NodeName))\n\t\t\t\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\"skipping Pod %s for Service %s/%s: Node %s Not Found\", pod.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, pod.Spec.NodeName))\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Skip pods on nodes with NoScheduleNoTraffic taints if PublishNotReadyAddresses is false\n\t\tif node != nil && !service.Spec.PublishNotReadyAddresses {\n\t\t\tfor _, taint := range node.Spec.Taints {\n\t\t\t\tif taint.Effect == corev1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic {\n\t\t\t\t\tlogger.Info(\"skipping Pod for Service, Node has NoScheduleNoTraffic taint\", \"pod\", klog.KObj(pod), \"service\", klog.KObj(service), \"node\", klog.KObj(node))\n\t\t\t\t\terrs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf(\"skipping Pod %s for Service %s/%s: Node %s has NoScheduleNoTraffic taint\", pod.Name, service.Namespace, service.Name, node.Name))\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tendpoint := podToEndpoint(pod, node, service, addressType)\n\t\tif len(endpoint.Addresses) > 0 {\n\t\t\tdesiredEndpointsByPortMap[epHash].Insert(&endpoint)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/reconciler.go"
}
Output
"File created successfully at: /workspace/reconciler.go"
60. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "// File: test/e2e/framework/node/resource.go (lines 419-435)\n// Updated toleratesTaintsWithNoScheduleNoExecuteEffects function\n\nfunc toleratesTaintsWithNoScheduleNoExecuteEffects(taints []v1.Taint, tolerations []v1.Toleration) bool {\n\tfilteredTaints := []v1.Taint{}\n\tfor _, taint := range taints {\n\t\tif taint.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoExecute || taint.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoSchedule || taint.Effect == v1.TaintEffectNoScheduleNoTraffic {\n\t\t\tfilteredTaints = append(filteredTaints, taint)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\ttoleratesTaint := func(taint v1.Taint) bool {\n\t\tfor _, toleration := range tolerations {\n\t\t\tif toleration.ToleratesTaint(&taint) {\n\t\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/resource.go"
}
Output
"File created successfully at: /workspace/resource.go"

Input
{
  "command": "for file in types_1.go types_2.go taints.go utils.go validation.go taint.go reconciler.go resource.go; do echo \"=== Checking $file ===\"; head -5 \"$file\"; echo; done",
  "description": "Display first few lines of each created file"
}
